null,
"customFields":
null,
"relatedTransactions":
[
],
"failures":
[
],
"history":
[
]
}
```
:::
## EPG merchant portal
### Create a transaction
1. Click the **Payments** menu item.
2. Click the **Transactions** tab.
3. Click the **New Transaction** button. The **New Transaction** form appears.
4. Enter the **Transaction Amount**.
5. Select the **Action** from the dropdown list. Options are the following:
- Capture for Settlement - Select this option to settle the transaction and charge the customer as soon as possible.
- Authorise - Select this option if the transaction has yet to be fulfilled and should be charged on a later date. For example, if you need to settle an order after it has been shipped to the customer, select this option. You may then capture this transaction for settlement later.
6. Enter the **Order Ref Number** for this transaction, if required. To generate a randomized order reference number, click the **Generate** button instead.
7. In the **Payment Method** section, click the **Pay with Card** button.
8. Enter the **Customer Name** and the **Email** where the receipt will be sent, if required.
9. To email the receipt, select the **Email Receipt** checkbox. The Email field will be required if this is selected.
10. To save the customer profile for future use, select the **Save customer profile** checkbox.
11. In the **Payment** section, enter the **Card Number** and the **Expiry Date** in MM/YY format.
12. Enter the card's **Security Code**. If this is not available, select the **Security Code Unavailable** checkbox instead.
13. If the security code is available and you want to verify the validity of the card details, click the **Verify Card** button.
14. To enter the customer's billing address, do the following:
a. Toggle the **Billing Address** button. Additional fields appear.
b. Select the **Country** from the dropdown list.
c. Enter the **Address Line**, **Town/City**, and **Postal Code** in the corresponding fields.
15. To enter the customer's shipping address, do the following:
a. Toggle the **Shipping Address** button. Additional fields appear.
b. If the shipping address is the same as the billing address, select the **Same as Billing** checkbox and proceed to step 16.
c. Select the **Country** from the dropdown list.
d. Enter the **Address Line**, **Town/City**, and **Postal Code** in the corresponding fields.
16. Click the **Process Transaction** button to proceed with the transaction. The transaction details will appear after the transaction is processed.
:::::notice{note}
Depending on your account settings, you may need to select a currency for the transaction amount. If your account is set to use only one currency, the default currency will be used
:::::

_source.title: Void
_source.genAiContent:
# elavon-payment-gateway
## Void transactions
Voiding a transaction cancels a sale or a refund that has not yet been settled. It changes the transaction's status to voided. Only transactions that have authorized, captured for settlement, or held for review statuses can be voided. For transactions that have already been settled, you may only perform a refund on them, if required. For more information, see the :magic-link[Refund transaction]{id="refund_transaction"} page.
## API integration
For more request and response examples, and for the complete and detailed list of parameters, see the :magic-link[Transactions]{id="api-reference#tag/Transactions"} resource.
### Create a transaction
1. Send a Create Transaction POST request to the **/transactions** endpoint.
2. In your request, set the following parameters:
-type - The transaction type. This is required. Set this field to "void".
-parentTransaction - The transaction resource URL. This is required.
-shopperInteraction - The type of interaction with the customer that generated this transaction. Valid values are the following: ecommerce (default), mailOrder, telephoneOrder, merchantInitiated.
3. Wait for the response from the /transactions endpoint. In the response, take note of the following:
-id - The transaction resource ID. This is used for retrieving and updating the transaction.
-href - The transaction resource URL.
-isAuthorized - Indicates whether the transaction is authorized. Voids are authorized by the gateway.
:::::notice{note}
A transaction can only be voided if the value of its **state** parameter is one of the following: *authorized*, *captured*, or *heldForReview*. Transactions with a state other than those cannot be voided and will return an error response if a void is attempted on them.
:::::
### Examples
The following is an example of a JSON request for voiding a transaction.
```json
POST "https://uat.api.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/transactions"
{
"type": "void",
"parentTransaction": "https://uat.api.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/transactions/b3hmbytmjjj3cg64y24p6dffpyy6",
"shopperInteraction": "telephoneOrder"

## EPG merchant portal
In the EPG merchant portal, voiding a transaction can only be performed by users with a 'Manager of Admin' role.
### Void a transaction
1. Click the **Payments** menu item.
2. Click the **Transactions** tab.
3. Select the transaction that has not yet been settled that you want to void. The corresponding transaction info appears.
4. Click the **Void** button. A confirmation dialog box appears.
5. Click the **Void** button to proceed.
### Void a transaction from a customer's profile
1. Click the **Customers** menu item.
2. Select the customer associated with the transaction. The corresponding customer profile appears.
3. In the **Transaction History** section, select the transaction that has not yet been settled that you want to void. The corresponding transaction info appears.
4. See steps 4 to 5 under Void a Transaction section.

_source.title: Refund
_source.genAiContent:
# elavon-payment-gateway
## Refunds
Refunding a transaction returns the money to the customer. You can perform either a full or partial refund transaction. Only transactions that have already been settled can be refunded. For transactions that have not been settled, you may only perform a void on them, if required. For more information, see the :magic-link[void transaction]{id="void-transaction"} page.
## API integration
For more request and response examples, and for the complete and detailed list of parameters, see the :magic-link[Transactions]{id="api-reference#tag/Transactions"} resource.
### Create transaction
1. Send a Create Transaction POST request to the **/transactions** endpoint.
2. In your request, set the following parameters:
- type - The transaction type. This is required. Set this field to "refund".
- total - The total amount of the transaction. This is only required when processing a partial refund. It contains the fields below.
- amount - The actual value of the transaction. This is required.
- currencyCode - The ISO 4217 three-letter currency code. This is required.
- parentTransaction - The transaction resource URL. This is required.
- shopperInteraction - The type of interaction with the customer that generated this transaction. Valid values are the following: ecommerce (default), mailOrder, telephoneOrder, merchantInitiated.
3. Wait for the response from the **/transactions** endpoint. In the response, take note of the following:
- id - The transaction resource ID. This is used for retrieving and updating the transaction.
- href - The transaction resource URL.
- isAuthorized - Indicates whether the transaction is authorized. Refunds are authorized by the gateway.
### Example
The following is an example of a JSON request for a partial refund.
:::code-block
```json
POST "https://uat.api.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/transactions"
{
"type": "refund",
"total":
{
"amount":
"5.00",
"currencyCode":
"EUR"
},
"parentTransaction": "https://uat.api.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/transactions/8vdmrrrdkthfgkvqwpvjwcrvh82k",
"shopperInteraction": "telephoneOrder"
}
```
:::

## EPG merchant portal
In the EPG merchant portal, refunding a transaction can only be performed by users with a Manager or Admin role.
### Refund a transaction
1. Click the **Payments** menu item.
2. Click the **Transactions** tab.
3. Select the settled transaction that you want to refund. The corresponding transaction info appears.
4. Click the **Issue Refund** button. The **Issue Refund** window appears.
5. Enter the **Amount** to be refunded to the customer.
6. Review the customer's **Email** where the receipt will be sent and edit, if required. Note that this will not update the email on the customer's record.
7. To email the receipt, select the **Email Receipt** checkbox. The Email field will be required if this is selected.
8. Click the **Process Refund** button to proceed.
### Refund a transaction from a customer's profile
1. Click the **Customers** menu item.
2. Select the customer associated with the transaction. The corresponding customer profile appears.
3. In the **Transaction History** section, select the settled transaction that you want to refund. The corresponding transaction info appears.
4. Follow steps 4 to 8 under the 'Refund a transaction' section above.

_source.title: Apple Pay
_source.genAiContent:
# elavon-payment-gateway
## Apple Pay
To use Apple Pay on HPP redirect implementation, no further integration work is required as registration is handled by Elavon Payment Gateway. You only need to enable Apple Pay from the **Payments Settings** page in the EPG merchant portal.
To use Apple Pay on HPP lightbox or hosted payment fields, you need to follow the steps to [register your domain](#register-your-domain) and enable Apple Pay from the Payments Settings page in the EPG merchant portal..
By enabling Apple Pay, you agree that you have read and consented to the [Apple Pay Web Merchant Terms and Conditions](https://developer.apple.com/terms/apple-pay-web/).
If you are not using EPG's hosted payments page, you need to follow the instructions below to enable Apple Pay on your website. To use Apple Pay on your website, take note of the following:
- You must comply with the [Apple Pay guidelines](https://developer.apple.com/apple-pay/acceptable-use-guidelines-for-websites/), [Apple Pay Identity Guidelines](https://developer.apple.com/apple-pay/marketing/), and [Apple Pay on the Web Human Interface Guidelines](https://developer.apple.com/design/human-interface-guidelines/apple-pay/overview/introduction/).
- Your domains that will host the Apple Pay button must be served over HTTPS. Review the [Apple Pay Server Settings](http://developer.apple.com/documentation/apple_pay_on_the_web/setting_up_your_server).
- Your domains must have a valid SSL certificate.
- Your domains that will host the Apple Pay button must be registered with Apple before Apple Pay is enabled.
- Review [Apple’s Developer Documentation](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/apple_pay_on_the_web) for Apple Pay to see the full set of requirements and best practices.
## Register your domain
1. Create two files with the name of ‘*apple-developer-merchantid-domain-association*’ under a folder named ‘*.well-known*’ in your domain. One of the files should have the *.txt* extension and the other one should have no extension. An example for the file with the *.txt* extension is:
`https://yourdomain/.well-known/apple-developer-merchantid-domain-association.txt`
2. Host the demo and production validation files to your server by doing the following:
a. Go to the validation file path. The following are the paths for the demo and production validation files:
- **Demo validation file**:
- **Production validation file**:
b. Highlight and copy the entire content of the validation file and paste it to the two files (with and without *.txt* extension) created under the ‘*.well-known*’ folder.
c. Remove and/or disable any captchas at the URLs hosting the two files.
d. The files must not be configured as attachments for download on your web server. Instead, the file contents must be displayed when navigating to the URL
3. Register the domains and subdomains that will display the Apple Pay button for payments with EPG. :magic-link[Contact the helpdesk]{id="overview#support"} for further assistance.
## Setting up Apple Pay on your website
1. Follow the steps to [register your domain](#register-your-domain).
2. Obtain the basic auth EPG API endpoint credentials. For more information, see the :magic-link[Authentication section]{id="api-reference#section/Overview/Authentication"} of the EPG API Reference.
3. From your website within the *onvalidatemerchant* Apple Pay JS event handler, call the EPG API endpoint to obtain an Apple Pay payment session. See the example for [obtaining Apple Pay payment session](#obtain-apple-pay-payment-session). Also check out [Apple Pay's documentation on onvalidatemerchant](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/apple_pay_on_the_web/applepaysession/1778021-onvalidatemerchant) for more information.
4. Once the Apple Payment session is obtained, send it as an argument in the Apple Pay *completeMerchantValidation* function. See [Apple Pay's documentation on completeMerchantValidation](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/apple_pay_on_the_web/applepaysession/1778015-completemerchantvalidation) for more information.
5.
Apple will call the *onpaymentauthorized* event handler with the Apple Pay token as an event argument. See [Apple Pay's documentation on onpaymentauthorized](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/apple_pay_on_the_web/applepaysession/1778020-onpaymentauthorized) for more information.
6. Within *onpaymentauthorized*, the Apple Pay token should be passed in a call to the EPG API endpoint to create an Apple Pay payment resource. The EPG API endpoint will respond with an Apple Pay payment. See the example for [creating an Apple Pay payment resource](#create-an-apple-pay-payment-resource).
7. Use the ID of the returned Apple Pay payment from the previous step in a call to create a transaction for processing. See the example for [creating a transaction using Apple Pay payment ID](#create-a-transaction-using-apple-pay-payment-id).
## Examples
### Obtain Apple Pay payment session
**Request body**
:::code-block
```json
POST https://uat.api.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/apple-pay-payment-sessions
{
"account":"7btv9vr8cq3nq8r7p4wjmjkk58bt",
"initiativeContext":""
}
```
:::
**Response body**
:::code-block
```json
{
"merchant": "https://uat.api.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/merchants/7btv9vr8cq3nq8r7p4wjmjkk58bt",
"account": "https://uat.api.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/accounts/7btv9vr8cq3nq8r7p4wjmjkk58bt",
"displayName": "",
"initiativeContext": "",
"paymentSession": ""
}
```
:::
### Create an Apple Pay payment resource
**Request body**
:::code-block
```json
POST https://uat.api.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/apple-pay-payments
{
"token": "",
"card": {
"billTo": {
"company": "US_ElavonBank",
"primaryPhone": "4041234567",
"alternatePhone": "8885555555",
"fax": "123456789",
"email": "email@domain.com",
"street1": "123 Main Street",
"street2": "SW",
"city": "New York",
"region": "NA",
"postalCode": "",
"countryCode": "USA"
}
},
"customReference": "customReference",
"customFields": {"name1":"value1", "name2":"value2"}
}
```
:::
**Response body**
:::code-block
```json
{
"href": "https://uat.api.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/apple-pay-payments/8jmkgdx63t77xqtwq6j2f27cykrd",
"id": "8jmkgdx63t77xqtwq6j2f27cykrd",

},

},

}
}
```
:::
### Create a transaction using Apple Pay payment ID
**Request body**
:::code-block
```json
POST https://uat.api.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/transactions

"total": {
"amount": "20.00",
"currencyCode": "USD"
},

}
```
:::
**Response body**
:::code-block
```json

"id": "79t3cg44993qm8ftb8769c37w8kg",

Overview
_source.genAiContent:
# elavon-payment-gateway
## Overview
Elavon Payment Gateway can manage your wallet for you through the :magic-link[Shoppers]{id="api-reference#tag/Shoppers"} and :magic-link[Stored Cards]{id="api-reference#tag/Stored-Cards"} resources. It can also automate your scheduled payments through the :magic-link[Plans]{id="api-reference#tag/Plans"} and :magic-link[Subscriptions]{id="api-reference#tag/Subscriptions"} resources. However, if you want to manage your wallet by yourself, there is a way on how to do this by assigning values to certain parameters in the :magic-link[Transactions]{id="api-reference#tag/Transactions"} resource.
The *credentialOnFileType* and *credentialOnFileData* parameters of the :magic-link[Transactions]{id="api-reference#tag/Transactions"} resource must be used to manage your own wallet. The value for *credentialOnFileData* is not initially available. You must first create the initial transaction and set the *credentialOnFileType* to any of the following:
- **subscription** - A subscription is a series of scheduled transactions with a fixed amount. This is commonly used by businesses that collect payments for their products or services in multiple installments. An example is a monthly membership to an application that gives you access to its features.
- **recurring** - Recurring payments are a series of scheduled transactions with a variable amounts. This is commonly used by businesses that collect payments for their products or services in multiple installments. An example is monthly payments to a utility company where payments are expected monthly but the amount to be paid varies depending on usage.
- **unscheduled** - There are cases when a cardholder would make multiple unscheduled transactions using the same credentials, or when a merchant may need to create unscheduled transactions using a customer's card stored on file. Some examples are:
- an online shopping site where purchases are made without a fixed timetable but with the same card.
- a fee that is incurred after an initial transaction is performed, such as no-show or cancellation fees in an appointment system.
Once you receive the response for the initial transaction, take note of the *credentialOnFileData* value. This value will be used in the succeeding transactions for the selected *credentialOnFileType*.
Aside from setting up the initial transaction, note that you are responsible for storing, managing, and securing the *credentialOnFiledata*, card details, and payment schedule, if required. EPG will not store or manage these details on your behalf if you are managing the wallet by yourself. It is therefore recommended to allow EPG to manage your wallet. For more information regarding EPG-managed wallets, see the following pages:
- :magic-link[Stored Shoppers and Stored Cards]{id="stored-shoppers-and-cards"}
- :magic-link[Create Plans and Subscriptions]{id="create-plans-and-subscriptions"}
For managed wallets, see the following pages for detailed steps on setting up the initial and succeeding transactions for each *credentialOnFileType*:
- :magic-link[Scheduled Payments]{id="scheduled-payments"}
- :magic-link[Card on File Transactions]{id="cof-transactions"}

_source.title: Scheduled payments
_source.genAiContent:
# elavon-payment-gateway
## Scheduled payments
For more request and response examples, and for the complete and detailed list of parameters, see the
:magic-link[Transactions]{id="api-reference#tag/Transactions"} resource.
## Create scheduled payments with the same amount
### Initial transaction
For managed wallets, the steps below outline the initial transaction for managing a series of scheduled payments with the same amount:
1. Send a Create Transaction POST request to the **/transactions** endpoint.
2. In your request, set the following parameters:
- total - The total amount of the transaction. It contains the fields below:
- amount - The actual value of the transaction. This is required.
- currencyCode - The ISO 4217 three-letter currency code. This is required.
- shopperInteraction - The type of interaction with the customer that generated this transaction. The following are valid values: *ecommerce* (default), *mailOrder*, *telephoneOrder*. Note that using the default option will not authorize if the 3DS details are not provided.
- credentialOnFileType - Credential on file type. Set to "subscription".
- card - The customer's card details. It contains the fields below:
- holderName - The customer's full name.
- number - The cardholder's personal account number. This is required and the value is not returned.
- expirationMonth - The card's expiration month. This is required.
- expirationYear - The card's expiration year. This is required.
- securityCode - The card's security code. This is required and the value is not returned.
- billTo - The customer's billing address. It contains the fields below:
- street1 - The first address line.
- street2 - The second address line.
- city - The town or city.
- postalCode - The zip or postal code. This is required for UK merchants.
- countryCode - The ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3 country code.
3. Wait for the response from the **/transactions** endpoint. In the response, take note of the following:
- id - The transaction resource ID. This is used for retrieving and updating the transaction.
- href - The transaction resource URL.
- credentialOnFileData - The value returned from the initial use of a card in an managed wallet. This value must be saved and used in the succeeding transactions for the subscription.
The following is an example of a JSON request for the initial transaction of a EUR 1.00 monthly subscription using John Doe's card.
:::code-block
```json
POST "https://uat.api.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/transactions"
{
"total":
{
"amount":
"1.00",
"currencyCode":
"EUR"
},
"shopperInteraction": "telephoneOrder",
"credentialOnFileType": "subscription",
"card":
{
"holderName":
"John Doe",
"number":
"5100.0800.0004.1251",
"expirationMonth":
1,
"expirationYear":
2025
}
}
```
:::
The following is an example of a successful JSON response to the request above.
:::code-block
```json

The steps below outline the succeeding transactions for a series of scheduled payments with the same amount:
1. Send a Create Transaction POST request to the **/transactions** endpoint.
2. In your request, set the following parameters:
- total - The total amount of the transaction. It contains the fields below:
- amount - The actual value of the transaction. This is required.
- currencyCode - The ISO 4217 three-letter currency code. This is required.
- shopperInteraction - The type of interaction with the customer that generated this transaction. Set to "merchantInitiated".
- credentialOnFileType - Credential on file type. Set to "subscription".
- credentialOnFileData - The value returned from the initial use of a card in an managed wallet.
- card - The customer's card details. It contains the fields below:
- holderName - The customer's full name.
- number - The cardholder's personal account number. This is required and the value is not returned.
- expirationMonth - The card's expiration month. This is required.
- expirationYear - The card's expiration year. This is required.
- securityCode - The card's security code. This is required and the value is not returned.
- billTo - The customer's billing address. It contains the fields below:
- street1 - The first address line.
- street2 - The second address line.
- city - The town or city.
- postalCode - The zip or postal code. This is required for UK merchants.
- countryCode - The ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3 country code.
3. Wait for the response from the **/transactions** endpoint. In the response, take note of the following:
- id - The transaction resource ID. This is used for retrieving and updating the transaction.
- href - The transaction resource URL.
The following is an example of a JSON request for the succeeding transaction of a EUR 1.00 monthly subscription using John Doe's card.
:::code-block
```json
POST "https://uat.api.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/transactions"
{
"total":
{
"amount":
"1.00",
"currencyCode":
"EUR"
},
"shopperInteraction": "merchantInitiated",
"credentialOnFileType": "subscription",
"credentialOnFileData": "MMSI102181

}

```

## Create scheduled payments with variable amounts
### Initial transaction
For managed wallets, the steps below outline the initial transaction for a series of scheduled payments with variable amounts:
1. Send a Create Transaction POST request to the **/transactions** endpoint.
2. In your request, set the following parameters:
- total - The total amount of the transaction. It contains the fields below:
- amount - The actual value of the transaction. This is required.
- currencyCode - The ISO 4217 three-letter currency code. This is required.
- shopperInteraction - The type of interaction with the customer that generated this transaction. Valid values are the following: ecommerce (default), mailOrder, telephoneOrder. Note that using the default option will not authorize if the 3DS details are not provided.
- credentialOnFileType - Credential on file type. Set to "recurring".

- number - The cardholder's personal account number. This is required and the value is not returned.
- expirationMonth - The card's expiration month. This is required.
- expirationYear - The card's expiration year. This is required.
- securityCode - The card's security code. This is required and the value is not returned.
- billTo - The customer's billing address. It contains the fields below:
- street1 - The first address line.
- street2 - The second address line.
- city - The town or city.
- postalCode - The zip or postal code. This is required for UK merchants.
- countryCode - The ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3 country code.
3. Wait for the response from the **/transactions** endpoint. In the response, take note of the following:
- id - The transaction resource ID. This is used for retrieving and updating the transaction.
- href - The transaction resource URL.
- credentialOnFileData - The value returned from the initial use of a card in an managed wallet. This value must be saved and used in the succeeding transactions for the recurring payments.

_source.title: Card on file transactions
_source.genAiContent:
# elavon-payment-gateway
## Card on File Transactions
For more request and response examples, and for the complete and detailed list of parameters, see the :magic-link[Transactions]{id="api-reference#tag/Transactions"} resource.
## Create cardholder-initiated transaction from card on file
### Initial transaction
For integrator-managed wallets, the steps below outline the initial transaction for unscheduled cardholder-initiated transactions with their card on file:
1. Send a Create Transaction POST request to the **/transactions** endpoint.
2. In your request, set the following parameters:
- total - The total amount of the transaction. It contains the fields below:
- amount - The actual value of the transaction. This is required.
- currencyCode - The ISO 4217 three-letter currency code. This is required.
- shopperInteraction - The type of interaction with the customer that generated this transaction. Valid values are the following: ecommerce (default), mailOrder, telephoneOrder. Note that using the default option will not authorize if the 3DS details are not provided.
- credentialOnFileType - Credential on file type. Set to "unscheduled".
- card - The customer's card details. It contains the fields below:
- holderName - The customer's full name.
- number - The cardholder's personal account number. This is required and the value is not returned.
- expirationMonth - The card's expiration month. This is required.
- expirationYear - The card's expiration year. This is required.
- securityCode - The card's security code. This is required and the value is not returned.
- billTo - The customer's billing address. It contains the fields below:
- street1 - The first address line.
- street2 - The second address line.
- city - The town or city.
- postalCode - The zip or postal code. This is required for UK merchants.
- countryCode - The ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3 country code.
3. Wait for the response from the **/transactions** endpoint. In the response, take note of the following:
- id - The transaction resource ID. This is used for retrieving and updating the transaction.
- href - The transaction resource URL.
- credentialOnFileData - The value returned from the initial use of a card in an integrator-managed wallet. This value must be saved and used in any future transactions initiated by the cardholder.
The following is an example of a JSON request for the initial unscheduled transaction of USD 4.00 using John Doe's card.
:::code-block
```json
POST "https://uat.api.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/transactions"
{
"total":
{
"amount":
"4.00",
"currencyCode":
"USD"
},
"shopperInteraction": "telephoneOrder",
"credentialOnFileType": "unscheduled",
"card":
{
"holderName":
"John Doe",
"number":
"5100.0800.0004.1251",
"expirationMonth":
1,
"expirationYear":
2025
}
}
```
:::

### Subsequent transactions
The steps below outline any future unscheduled transactions initiated by the cardholder with their card on file:
1. Send a Create Transaction POST request to the **/transactions** endpoint.
2. In your request, set the following parameters:
- total - The total amount of the transaction. It contains the fields below:
- amount - The actual value of the transaction. This is required.
- currencyCode - The ISO 4217 three-letter currency code. This is required.
- shopperInteraction - The type of interaction with the customer that generated this transaction. Valid values are the following: ecommerce (default), mailOrder, telephoneOrder. Note that using the default option will not authorize if the 3DS details are not provided.
- credentialOnFileType - Credential on file type. Set to "unscheduled".
- credentialOnFileData - The value returned from the initial use of a card in an integrator-managed wallet.
- card - The customer's card details. It contains the fields below:
- holderName - The customer's full name.
- number - The cardholder's personal account number. This is required and the value is not returned.
- expirationMonth - The card's expiration month. This is required.
- expirationYear - The card's expiration year. This is required.
- securityCode - The card's security code. This is required and the value is not returned.
- billTo - The customer's billing address. It contains the fields below:
- street1 - The first address line.
- street2 - The second address line.
- city - The town or city.
- postalCode - The zip or postal code. This is required for UK merchants.
- countryCode - The ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3 country code.
3. Wait for the response from the **/transactions** endpoint. In the response, take note of the following:
- id - The transaction resource ID. This is used for retrieving and updating the transaction.
- href - The transaction resource URL.
The following is an example of a JSON request for the succeeding unscheduled transaction of USD 5.00 initiated by the cardholder using John Doe's card.
:::code-block
```json

{
"total":
{
"amount":
"5.00",
"currencyCode":
"USD"
},

"credentialOnFileType": "unscheduled",
"credentialOnFileData": "MMSI060802
0412

## Create merchant-initiated transaction from card on file
### Initial transaction
For integrator-managed wallets, the steps below outline the initial transaction for unscheduled merchant-initiated transactions with the customer's card on file:
1. Send a Create Transaction POST request to the **/transactions** endpoint.
2. In your request, set the following parameters:
- total - The total amount of the transaction. It contains the fields below:
- amount - The actual value of the transaction. This is required.
- currencyCode - The ISO 4217 three-letter currency code. This is required.
- shopperInteraction - The type of interaction with the customer that generated this transaction. Valid values are the following: ecommerce (default), mailOrder, telephoneOrder. Note that using the default option will not authorize if the 3DS details are not provided.
- credentialOnFileType - Credential on file type. Set to "unscheduled".
- card - The customer's card details. It contains the fields below:
- holderName - The customer's full name.
- number - The cardholder's personal account number. This is required and the value is not returned.
- expirationMonth - The card's expiration month. This is required.
- expirationYear - The card's expiration year. This is required.
- securityCode - The card's security code. This is required and the value is not returned.
- billTo - The customer's billing address. It contains the fields below:
- street1 - The first address line.
- street2 - The second address line.
- city - The town or city.
- postalCode - The zip or postal code. This is required for UK merchants.
- countryCode - The ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3 country code.
3. Wait for the response from the **/transactions** endpoint. In the response, take note of the following:
- id - The transaction resource ID. This is used for retrieving and updating the transaction.
- href - The transaction resource URL.
- credentialOnFileData - The value returned from the initial use of a card in an integrator-managed wallet. This value must be saved and used in any future transactions initiated by the merchant.

"total":
{
"amount":
"6.00",
"currencyCode":
"USD"
},
"shopperInteraction": "telephoneOrder",
"credentialOnFileType": "unscheduled",
"card":
{

_source.title: Payment links
_source.genAiContent:
# elavon-payment-gateway
## Payment links
Payment links can be sent to customers for them to make payments online. These links send the shopper to your hosted payments page to make a one-time payment. Payment links can be sent by either using the EPG email template or as a URL pasted into an email or other online communication form you use.
Payment links have a set lifespan you can control. The expiration date of a payment link can be set for up to one year from its creation date. Once a payment link has expired, it can no longer be used, and a new link will be needed to complete the payment, if necessary.
## API integration
For more request and response examples, and for the complete and detailed list of parameters, see the :magic-link[Payment Links]{id="api-reference#tag/Payment-Links"} resource.
### Create payment link
1. Send a Create Shopper POST request to the **/payment-links** endpoint.
2. In your request, set the following parameters:
- expiresAt - The expiration date and time of the payment link. The date and time must be within one year of the current date. This is required.
- doCapture - The action that will be passed on to the transaction. If set to true, the transaction will capture for settlement. If set to false, the transaction will authorize only.
- total - The total amount of the transaction. This is required. It contains the fields below:
- amount - The actual value of the transaction. This is required.
- currencyCode - The ISO 4217 three-letter currency code. This is required.
- shopperEmailAddress - The customer's email address where the receipt will be sent.
- shopper - The shopper resource URL.
3. Wait for the response from the **/payment-links** endpoint. In the response, take note of the following:
- id - The payment link resource ID. This is used for retrieving and updating the payment link.
- url - The payment link. This is the link sent to the customer for them to proceed with the transaction.
#### Example
The following is an example of a JSON request for creating a payment link for a USD 10.00 transaction that expires on June 30, 2022 at 12:00 PM.
:::code-block
```json
POST "https://uat.api.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/payment-links"
{
"expiresAt": "2022-06-30T12:00:00.000Z",
"total":
{
"amount":
"10.00",
"currencyCode":
"USD"
}
}
```
:::
The following is an example of a successful JSON response to the request above.
:::code-block
```json
{
"href":
"https://uat.api.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/payment-links/2vtdbjxphdj9dh8pfgt3kffwbfcj",
"id":
"2vtdbjxphdj9dh8pfgt3kffwbfcj",
"merchant":
"https://uat.api.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/merchants/XrDXRBh9YHxwqQTj2Cmq7j49",
"account":
"https://uat.api.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/accounts/XrDXRBh9YHxwqQTj2Cmq7j49",
"url":
"https://uat.hpp.converge.eu.elavonaws.com?pl=2vtdbjxphdj9dh8pfgt3kffwbfcj",
"returnUrl":
null,
"createdAt":
"2022-06-01T17:39:36.058Z",
"createdBy":
null,
"modifiedAt":
"2022-06-01T17:39:36.058Z",
"expiresAt":
"2022-06-30T12:00:00.000Z",
"cancelledAt":
null,
"cancelledBy":
null,
"doCancel":
false,
"doCapture":
null,
"description":
null,
"total":
{
"amount":
"10.00",
"currencyCode":
"USD"
},
"debtorAccount":
null,
"orderReference":
null,
"shopperEmailAddress":
null,
"shopper":
null,
"customReference":
null,
"customFields":
null
}
```
:::
### Update payment link
1. Send an Update Payment Link POST request to **/payment-links/{id}**, where **id** is the payment link's resource id. Below is an example of a complete URL where to send the request:
`https://uat.api.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/payment-links/2vtdbjxphdj9dh8pfgt3kffwbfcj`
2. In your request, set the following parameter:
- doCancel - This determines the status of the payment link. If set to true, the payment link is cancelled. If set to false (default), the payment link remains active.
- cancelledBy - The user who performed the cancellation. This is an optional parameter that you can use if you want to capture this detail.
3. Wait for the response from the **/payment-links** endpoint. In the response, take note of the following:
- cancelledAt - The date and time when the payment link was cancelled.
#### Examples
The following is an example of a JSON request for cancelling a payment link.
:::code-block
```json
POST "https://uat.api.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/payment-links/2vtdbjxphdj9dh8pfgt3kffwbfcj"
{
"doCancel": true
}
```
:::
The following is an example of a successful JSON response to the request above.
:::code-block
```json
{
"href":
"https://uat.api.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/payment-links/2vtdbjxphdj9dh8pfgt3kffwbfcj",
"id":
"2vtdbjxphdj9dh8pfgt3kffwbfcj",
"merchant":
"https://uat.api.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/merchants/XrDXRBh9YHxwqQTj2Cmq7j49",
"account":
"https://uat.api.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/accounts/XrDXRBh9YHxwqQTj2Cmq7j49",

```
:::
## EPG merchant portal
Before you can create payment links, the pay-by-link feature must be first enabled. Go to the Pay-by-Link page in Settings to enable the feature. You can also configure pay-by-link settings from this page, such as the default link expiration, default email subject line, and various appearance settings.
### Create a payment link
1. See steps 1 to 6 under :magic-link[Create a Transaction]{id="sale_transaction#create-a-transaction"} in the Sale Transaction page.
2. In the **Payment Method** section, click the **Pay-by-Link** button.
3. Select the **Link Format**:
- Send using EPG email template - Select this option to send the payment link to the customer's email. EPG will use a pre-formatted email template when sending the payment link.
- Generate URL to copy/paste - Select this option to generate a URL for the payment link. This option gives you more flexibility on how to deliver the payment link to the customer. For example, the payment link could be embedded in your own custom email template, sent through SMS, etc.
4. In the **Link Expiration** section, enter the **Expiration Date**. You may also click the calendar icon to select the date. Note that the expiration date cannot be earlier than the current date and must be within one year from the current date.
5. Enter the **Time** when the payment link will expire on the selected expiration date. You may also click the time icon to select the time details.
6. In the **Customer** section, do one of the following:
- Enter the **Customer Name** and **Email**. For more details, see steps 8 to 10 under :magic-link[Create a Transaction in the Sale Transaction]{id="sale_transaction#create-a-transaction"} page.
- Search for the customer profile. For more details, see step 2 under :magic-link[Create a Transaction in the Stored Shoppers and Stored Cards]{id="stored-shoppers-and-cards#create-a-transaction"} page.
7. If the link format selected is Send using EPG email template, do the following:
1. To change the default email subject line, toggle the **Override default email subject line** button and enter the new **Email Subject Line**.
2. To add a custom message to the email, toggle the **Add email message** button and enter the **Email Message** that you want to add regarding the payment link.
8. Depending on the selected link format, click the **Send Email** or **Generate Link** button to proceed.
9. If the selected link format is Generate URL to copy/paste, the **Payment Link URL** window appears. Click the **Copy URL and Close Window** button to proceed.
### Cancel a payment link
1. Click the **Payments** menu item.
2. Click the **Pay-by-Link** tab.
3. Select the payment link that you want to cancel. Ensure that the status of the payment link you are selecting is active. The corresponding payment link info appears.
4. Click the **Cancel** button. A confirmation dialog box appears.
5. Click the **Cancel Link** button to proceed.

_source.title: Create plans and subscriptions
_source.genAiContent:
# elavon-payment-gateway
## Create plans and subscriptions
Elavon Payment Gateway provides a set of features that allows users to handle recurring charges. Merchants are allowed to use a stored card resource to process each of their payment increments automatically when the interval between them has elapsed. If the merchant chooses to use resources provided by EPG (e.g., stored cards, plans, subscriptions, and shoppers), EPG will manage the payment processing schedule.
Implementing plans and subscriptions is ideal for the following:
- For businesses in the software as a service (SaaS) or service as a product (SaaP) markets that need to collect recurring payments from customers.
- For businesses that collect payments for their product or service in multiple installments.
## API integration
For more request and response examples, and for the complete and detailed list of parameters, see the :magic-link[Plans]{id="api-reference#tag/Plans"} and the :magic-link[Subscriptions]{id="api-reference#tag/Subscriptions"} API resources.
### Create plan
1. Send a Create Plan POST request to the **/plans** endpoint.
2. In your request, set the following parameters:
- name - The name of the plan. This is required.
- billingInterval - The time period between bills. This is required. It contains the following fields:
- timeUnit - The time unit used for the billing cycle. Valid values are the following: day, week, month, year.
- count - The number of times the time unit will repeat until the next billing cycle.
- total - The total amount for each bill. This does not include introductory bills. This is required. It contains the following fields:
- amount - The actual value of the bill.
- currencyCode - The ISO 4217 three-letter currency code.
- billCount - The total number of billing cycles. This includes any introductory bills.
- initialTotal - The introductory bill. It contains the following fields:
- amount - The actual value of the introductory bill.
- currencyCode - The ISO 4217 three-letter currency code.
- initialTotalBillCount - The number of times the introductory bill will be applied. Note that this is not added to the total number of billing cycles as that already accounts for it. It should also have a lower value than than the total number of billing cycles.
- isSubscribable - If set to true (default), new subscriptions can be added to the plan. Cancelled subscriptions can also be reactivated. If set to false, new subscriptions cannot be added and cancelled subscriptions cannot be reactivated.
3. Wait for the response from the **/plans** endpoint. In the response, take note of the following:
- id - The plan resource ID. This is used for retrieving and updating the plan.
- href - The plan resource URL. This is used for associating the plan with a subscription.
### Create subscription
1. Send a Create Subscription POST request to the **/subscriptions** endpoint.
2. In your request, set the following parameters:
- plan - The plan resource URL. This is obtained from a successful Create Plan response. This is required.
- doSendReceipt - If set to true, receipt for every transaction will be sent to the customer's email. If set to false, receipts will not be emailed.
- storedCard - The stored card resource URL. This must be associated with a shopper.
- storedAchPayment - An ACH payment that is stored for a shopper and may be used for recurring payments.
- surchargeAdvice - SurchargeAdvice resource URL for the remaining totals. Obtained through the create surchargeAdvice API call.
- initialSurchargeAdvice - SurchargeAdvice resource URL for the initial bill. Obtained through the create surchargeAdvice API call.
- billCount - The total number of bills. This can only be set if it is not yet defined in the plan associated with the subscription.
- timeZoneID - The IANA Timezone Database Name of the first billing date. It indicates exactly when to charge the card associated with the subscription. This is required.
- firstBillAt - The first billing date. This is required.
The request must contain either `storedCard` or `storedAchPayment`.
3. Wait for the response from the **/subscriptions** endpoint. In the response, take note of the following:
- id - The subscription resource ID. This is used for retrieving and updating the subscription.
- href - The subscription resource URL.
### Example
The following is an example of a JSON request for creating a one-year plan that includes a one-month introductory rate.
:::code-block
```json
POST "https://uat.api.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/plans"
{
"name": "Monthly Software License",
"billingInterval":
{
"timeUnit":
"month",
"count":
1
},
"total":
{
"amount":
"5.00",
"currencyCode":
"USD"
},
"billCount":
12,
"initialTotal":
{
"amount":
"2.50",
"currencyCode":
"USD"
},
"initialTotalBillCount":
1
}
```
:::
The following is an example of a successful JSON response to the request above.
:::code-block
```json
{
"href":
"https://uat.api.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/plans/ry2t4kh4gkrk3h7vq9wx36794gvp",
"id":
"ry2t4kh4gkrk3h7vq9wx36794gvp",
"createdAt":
"2022-04-04T19:52:00.274Z",
"modifiedAt":
"2022-04-04T19:52:00.274Z",
"deletedAt":
null,
"merchant":
"https://uat.api.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/merchants/XrDXRBh9YHxwqQTj2Cmq7j49",
"planList":
"https://uat.api.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/plan-lists/XrDXRBh9YHxwqQTj2Cmq7j49",
"name":
"Monthly Software License",
"description":
null,
"billingInterval":
{
"timeUnit":
"month",
"count":
1
},
"total":
{
"amount":
"5.00",
"currencyCode":
"USD"
},
"billCount":
12,
"initialTotal":
{
"amount":
"2.50",
"currencyCode":
"USD"
},
"initialTotalBillCount":
1,
"shopperStatement":
null,
"isSubscribable":
true,
"customReference":
null,
"customFields":
null
}
```
:::
The following is an example of a JSON request for adding a subscription to a plan for 12 billing cycles. This assumes that a stored card is already created. For more information, see the :magic-link[Create Stored Card in the Stored Shoppers and Stored Cards]{id="stored-shoppers-and-cards#create-stored-card"} page.
:::code-block
```json
POST "https://uat.api.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/subscriptions"
{
"plan": "https://uat.api.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/plans/ry2t4kh4gkrk3h7vq9wx36794gvp",
"doSendReceipt": true,
"storedCard": "https://uat.api.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/stored-cards/g9fw9kg2fjfcdm62rb2f38cqmx9m",
"timeZoneId": "Europe/London",
"firstBillAt": "2022-04-22"
}
```
:::
The following is an example of a successful JSON response to the request above.
:::code-block
```json
{
"href":
"https://uat.api.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/subscriptions/dtdfj7bg8jbj86jcd63c66fm949p",
"id":
"dtdfj7bg8jbj86jcd63c66fm949p",
"createdAt":
"2022-04-04T20:02:17.178Z",
"modifiedAt":
"2022-04-04T20:02:17.178Z",
"merchant":
"https://uat.api.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/merchants/XrDXRBh9YHxwqQTj2Cmq7j49",
"plan":
"https://uat.api.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/plans/ry2t4kh4gkrk3h7vq9wx36794gvp",
"shopper":
"https://uat.api.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/shoppers/pbrcccxy9pgk4qfdcbhf88w97hxv",
"account":
"https://uat.api.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/accounts/XrDXRBh9YHxwqQTj2Cmq7j49",
"debtorAccount":
null,
"doSendReceipt":
"true",
"storedCard":
"https://uat.api.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/stored-cards/g9fw9kg2fjfcdm62rb2f38cqmx9m",
"billCount":
12,
"timeZoneId":
"Europe/London",
"firstBillAt":
"2022-04-22",
"nextBillAt":
"2022-04-22",
"previousBillAt":
null,
"finalBillAt":
"2023-03-22",
"cancelRequestedAt":
null,
"cancelAfterBillNumber":
null,
"nextBillNumber":
1,
"subscriptionState":
"active",
"failureCount":
0,
"customReference":
null,
"customFields":
null
}
```
:::
## EPG merchant portal
### Create a plan
A plan provides a template that is used to pay for products and services and is created by the merchant. When a customer subscribes to a plan (e.g., a gym membership or online course), a merchant can set up the frequency of payments based on the customer's preferences.
1. Click the **Recurring** menu item.
2. Click the **Plans** tab.
3. Click the **New Plan** button. The **New Subscription Plan** window appears.
4. Enter the **Plan Name**.
5. In the **Pricing** section, enter the **Subscription Amount**.
6. If you have an initial rate that is different from the subscription amount, do the following:
a. Toggle the **Introductory Rate** button and enter the **Introductory Amount**.
b. Enter how many times the initial rate will be applied in the **Introductory Billing Period** field.
7. In the **Billing Frequency** section, select the **Billing Frequency** from the dropdown list. Depending on your selection, enter the number of times that the bill will occur in the fields that appear.
8. Select an option on how the plan will end. Options are the following:
- End subscription after X billing periods - If selected, enter the number of billing periods in the field.
- End when cancelled or scheduled - If selected, the plan will remain active until a subscription is cancelled or if an end date is specified when creating the subscription.
9. Click the **Create Plan** button to save your plan.
:::::notice{note}
Depending on your account settings, you may need to select a currency for the subscription amount and the introductory amount. If your account is set to use only one currency, the default currency will be used.
:::::
### Create a subscription
A subscription associates a shopper with a plan. The subscription provides details about how and when the shopper will be billed.
1. Click the **Recurring** menu item.
2. Click the **Subscriptions** tab.
3. Click the **New Subscription** button. The **New Subscription** form appears.
4. Select the **Subscription Plan** from the dropdown list.
5. In the **Scheduling** section, enter the date of the **First Payment** or click the calendar icon to select the date. Additional fields appear after making your selection and they vary depending on the selected subscription plan.
6. If the selected subscription plan has a fixed number of bills, the other fields will be populated and you may proceed to the next step. If the selected subscription plan has no fixed number of bills, toggle the **Specify subscription end terms** button to set an end date to the subscriptionand do one of the following:
- Select the **Choose end date** option then enter the **End Date**. You may also click the calendar icon to select the date.
- Select the **Set number of payments** option then enter the number of **Total Payments**.
Note that not if an end date is not set, the payment will recur indefinitely until it is manually cancelled.
7. In the **Customer** section, search for the subscribing customer by doing one of the following:
- In the **Search for a customer** field, enter a part of the customer's name and select the customer from the results.
- Select the customer from the **Search for a customer** dropdown list.
8. Select the card to be used from the **Payment Method** dropdown list.
9. To email the receipt to the customer, select the **Email Receipt** checkbox. A separate receipt will be emailed for each transaction.
10. Click the **Create Subscription** button to save the subscription.

_source.title: Manage plans and subscriptions
_source.genAiContent:
# elavon-payment-gateway
## Manage plans and subscriptions
## API integration
For more request and response examples, and for the complete and detailed list or parameters, see the :magic-link[Subscriptions]{id="api-reference#tag/Subscriptions"} and :magic-link[Plans]{id="api-reference#tag/Plans"} resources.
### Update a plan
1. Send an Update Plan POST request to **/plans/{id}**, where **id** is the plan's resource id. Below is an example of a complete URL where to send the request:
`https://uat.api.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/plans/ry2t4kh4gkrk3h7vq9wx36794gvp`
2. In your request, set the following parameter:
- isSubscribable - If set to true (default), new subscriptions can be added to the plan. Cancelled subscriptions can also be reactivated. If set to false, new subscriptions cannot be added and cancelled subscriptions cannot be reactivated.
3. Wait for the response from the /plans endpoint.
#### Example
The following is an example of a JSON request to lock a plan.
:::code-block
```json
POST "https://uat.api.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/plans/ry2t4kh4gkrk3h7vq9wx36794gvp"
{
"isSubscribable": false
}
```
:::
The following is an example of a successful JSON response to the request above.
:::code-block
```json
{
"href":
"https://uat.api.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/plans/ry2t4kh4gkrk3h7vq9wx36794gvp",
"id":
"ry2t4kh4gkrk3h7vq9wx36794gvp",
"createdAt":
"2022-04-04T19:52:00.274Z",
"modifiedAt":
"2022-04-04T20:11:53.678Z",
"deletedAt":
null,
"merchant":
"https://uat.api.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/merchants/XrDXRBh9YHxwqQTj2Cmq7j49",
"planList":
"https://uat.api.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/plan-lists/XrDXRBh9YHxwqQTj2Cmq7j49",
"name":
"Monthly Software License",
"description":
null,
"billingInterval":
{
"timeUnit":
"month",
"count":
1
},
"total":
{
"amount":
"5.00",
"currencyCode":
"USD"
},
"billCount":
12,
"initialTotal":
{
"amount":
"2.50",
"currencyCode":
"USD"
},
"initialTotalBillCount":
1,
"shopperStatement":
null,
"isSubscribable":
false,
"customReference":
null,
"customFields":
null
}
```
:::
### Update a subscription
1. Send an Update Subscription POST request to **/subscriptions/{id}**, where **id** is the subscription's resource id. Below is an example of a complete URL where to send the request:
`https://uat.api.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/subscriptions/dtdfj7bg8jbj86jcd63c66fm949p`
2. In your request, set the following parameter:
- cancelAfterBillNumber - The bill number after which no further billings will occur. If the value set for this field is equal to the current bill number, the subscription will be cancelled immediately.
3. Wait for the response from the /subscriptions endpoint.
#### Example
The following is an example of a JSON request for cancelling a subscription after the first bill.
:::code-block
```json
POST "https://uat.api.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/subscriptions/dtdfj7bg8jbj86jcd63c66fm949p"
{
"cancelAfterBillNumber": 1
}
```
:::
The following is an example of a successful JSON response to the request above.
:::code-block
```json
{
"href":
"https://uat.api.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/subscriptions/dtdfj7bg8jbj86jcd63c66fm949p",
"id":
"dtdfj7bg8jbj86jcd63c66fm949p",
"createdAt":
"2022-04-04T20:02:17.178Z",
"modifiedAt":
"2022-04-04T20:15:05.497Z",
"merchant":
"https://uat.api.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/merchants/XrDXRBh9YHxwqQTj2Cmq7j49",
"plan":
"https://uat.api.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/plans/ry2t4kh4gkrk3h7vq9wx36794gvp",
"shopper":
"https://uat.api.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/shoppers/pbrcccxy9pgk4qfdcbhf88w97hxv",
"account":
"https://uat.api.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/accounts/XrDXRBh9YHxwqQTj2Cmq7j49",
"debtorAccount":
null,
"doSendReceipt":
"true",
"storedCard":
"https://uat.api.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/stored-cards/g9fw9kg2fjfcdm62rb2f38cqmx9m",
"billCount":
12,
"timeZoneId":
"Europe/London",
"firstBillAt":
"2022-04-22",
"nextBillAt":
"2022-04-22",
"previousBillAt":
null,
"finalBillAt":
"2022-04-22",
"cancelRequestedAt":
"2022-04-04",
"cancelAfterBillNumber":
1,
"nextBillNumber":
1,
"subscriptionState":
"active",
"failureCount":
0,
"customReference":
null,
"customFields":
null
}
```
:::
### Delete plan
1. Send a Delete Plan DELETE request to **/plans/{id}**, where **id** is the plan's resource id. Below is an example of a complete URL where to send the request:
`https://uat.api.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/plans/ry2t4kh4gkrk3h7vq9wx36794gvp`
2. No response is sent if the request is successful. If an error is encountered, a response is sent from the /plans endpoint.
#### Example
The following is example of a JSON request for deleting a plan.
```json
DELETE "https://uat.api.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/plans/ry2t4kh4gkrk3h7vq9wx36794gvp"
```
## EPG merchant portal
### Lock a plan
A plan can be locked to prevent new subscriptions from being added and to prevent cancelled subscriptions from being reactivated. Locking plan does not impact currently active subscriptions.
1. Click the **Recurring** menu item.
2. Click the **Plans** tab.
3. Select the plan that you want to lock. The corresponding plan info appears.
4. Click the **Actions** button, then select **Lock Plan**. A confirmation dialog box appears.
5. Click the **Lock Plan** button to proceed.
:::::notice{note}
To unlock a plan, repeat the same steps and select **Unlock Plan** on Step 4 instead.
:::::
### Change the end date of a subscription
1. Click the **Recurring** menu item.
2. Click the **Subscriptions** tab.
3. Select the subscription whose end date you want to change. The corresponding subscription info appears.
4. Click the **Actions** button, then select **Change End Date**. The **Change Subscription End Date** window appears.
5. Enter the **New End Date**. You may also click the calendar icon to select the date. Note that the new end date cannot be later than the current end date.
6. Review the updated fields and click the **Save Changes** button to save the new end date.
:::::notice{note}
If the subscription you have selected was previously modified, the **Remove early end date** option will be available. You can select this option instead of entering a new date to reinstate the original end date that the subscription had.
:::::
### Cancel a subscription
1. Click the **Recurring** menu item.
2. Click the **Subscriptions** tab.
3. Select the subscription that you want to cancel. The corresponding subscription info appears.
4. Click the **Actions** button, then select **Cancel Subscription**. A confirmation dialog box appears.
5. Click the **Cancel Subscription** button to proceed with the cancellation.
### Change the end date or cancel a subscription from a customer's profile
1. Click the **Customers** menu item.
2. Select the customer associated with the subscription. The corresponding customer profile appears.
3. In the **Subscriptions** section, select the subscription whose end date you want to change or cancel. The corresponding subscription info appears.
4. See steps 4 to 6 under [Change the End Date of a Subscription section](#change-the-end-date-of-a-subscription) or steps 4 to 5 under [Cancel a Subscription section](#cancel-a-subscription).
### Delete a plan
A plan can be deleted if it is no longer needed. A plan cannot be deleted if it has active subscriptions associated with it. You must :magic-link[cancel subscriptions]{id="#cancel-a-subscription"} associated with a plan first before you can delete it.
1. Click the **Recurring** menu item.
2. Click the **Plans** tab.
3. Select the plan that you want to delete. The corresponding plan info appears.
4. Click the **Actions** button, then select **Delete Plan**. A confirmation dialog box appears.
5. Click the **Delete Plan** button to proceed.

_source.title: Change subscription email settings
_source.genAiContent:
# elavon-payment-gateway
## Change subscription email settings
A subscription's email settings can be changed (e.g., whether receipts for every transaction need to be emailed to the customer).
## API integration
For more request and response examples, and for the complete and detailed list of parameters, see the :magic-link[Subscriptions]{id="api-reference#tag/Subscriptions"} resource.
### Update subscription
1. Send an Update Subscription POST request to **/subscriptions/{id}**, where **id** is the subscription's resource id. Below is an example of a complete URL where to send the request:
`https://uat.api.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/subscriptions/dtdfj7bg8jbj86jcd63c66fm949p`
2. In your request, set the following parameter:
- doSendReceipt - If set to true, receipt for every transaction will be sent to the customer's email. If set to false, receipts will not be emailed.
3. Wait for the response from the /subscriptions endpoint.
### Example
The following is an example of a JSON request to send all transaction receipts to the customer's email.
:::code-block
```json
POST "https://uat.api.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/subscriptions/dtdfj7bg8jbj86jcd63c66fm949p"
{
"doSendReceipt": "true"
}
```
:::
The following is an example of a successful JSON response to the request above.
:::code-block
```json
{
"href":
"https://uat.api.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/subscriptions/dtdfj7bg8jbj86jcd63c66fm949p",

"dtdfj7bg8jbj86jcd63c66fm949p",

"billCount":
12,
"timeZoneId":
"Europe/London",

## EPG merchant portal
### Change the email settings of a subscription
1. Click the **Recurring** menu item.
2. Click the **Subscriptions** tab.
3. Select the subscription whose email settings you want to change. The corresponding subscription info appears.
4. Click the **Actions** button, then select **Edit Subscription**. The **Edit Subscription** window appears.
5. Select or deselect the **Email Receipt** checkbox. If selected, a separate receipt will be emailed for each transaction.
6. Click the **Save Changes** button to save the new email settings.
### Change the email settings of a subscription from a customer's profile
1. Click the **Customers** menu item.
2. Select the customer associated with the subscription. The corresponding customer profile appears.
3. In the **Subscriptions** section, select the subscription whose email settings you want to change. The corresponding subscription info appears.
4. See steps 4 to 6 under [Change the Email Settings a Subscription section](#change-the-email-settings-of-a-subscription).

_source.title: Overview
_source.genAiContent:
# elavon-payment-gateway
## Hosted payments overview
Elavon Payment Gateway offers hosted integration methods with redirect, lightbox modals, and payment fields. Because the payment is processed on Elavon's secure servers, you don't have to handle sensitive card data directly, reducing your PCI scope.
A hosted payment page is the best option for businesses who want a checkout form that gathers information without a lengthy integration process. You can configure a shopper's experience with the hosted payments form using the following options:
- Hosted pages
- Redirect mode
- Lightbox mode
- Payment links
- Hosted Fields
## Hosted pages
With a hosted payment page, the Elavon Payment Gateway manages both the payment form and PCI compliance, ensuring secure transactions. You can choose between two display modes: redirect, which takes the customer to a separate payment page, or lightbox, which overlays the payment form on your site. Through the :magic-link[merchant portal]{id="create-account#merchant-portal-web-based-interface"} web-based interface, you can customize the appearance of the payment page to match your branding. For complete design control, consider using :magic-link[hosted fields]{id="hosted-payments-overview#hosted-fields"}, which allow you to embed secure payment input fields directly into your own custom form.
### Redirect
Hosted payment redirect should be used when you want a low coding solution that does not require PCI level 1 compliance. With redirect a shopper is moved from your server to an Elavon Payment Gateway hosted payment page. For more information on hosted payment redirect, see the :magic-link[redirect quickstart SDK]{id="redirect-sdk"} resource.
:::image{id="/content/dam/developer-portal-solutions/en-gb/elavon/media/images/products/elavon-payment-gateway/compare-redirect.png" alt="order summary to payment page redirect" max-width="702" max-height="380"}
:::
#### Payment links
Payment links are another way to present a redirect with some additional options. You can use this approach to send payment forms to your shoppers using emails, invoices, embeddable payment buttons, or any other service that you can send a link through. Clicking on a payment link brings the shopper to a payment page where they can enter their information and complete the checkout.
For more information on generating payment links using the API, see the :magic-link[payment links]{id="payment-links"} resource.
### Lightbox
Hosted payment lightbox should be used when you want a low coding solution that does not require PCI level 1 compliance. With a lightbox display, Elavon Payment Gateway opens a modal window 'over the top' of your website with a payment form inside the modal. Your shopper can enter payment information and check out while remaining within 'viewing distance' of your website. For more information on hosted lightbox, see the :magic-link[lightbox quickstart SDK]{id="lightbox-sdk"} resource.
:::image{id="/content/dam/developer-portal-solutions/en-gb/elavon/media/images/products/elavon-payment-gateway/compare-lightbox.png" alt="lightbox payment modal" max-width="480" max-height="424"}
:::
## Hosted fields
Hosted payment fields should be used when you want a medium coding solution that does not require PCI level 1 compliance. The hosted payment fields integration offers the ability to position and style individual payment inputs hosted by Elavon on your site to fully customize the checkout experience. For more information on hosted payment fields, see the :magic-link[fields quickstart SDK]{id="fields-quickstart-sdk"} resource.
:::image{id="/content/dam/developer-portal-solutions/en-gb/elavon/media/images/products/elavon-payment-gateway/compare-fields.png" alt="hosted fields payment page" max-width="500" max-height="330"}
:::

_source.title: Lightbox JS library overview
_source.genAiContent:
# elavon-payment-gateway
## Lightbox JS library
The Elavon Lightbox JS Library is a secure, embeddable payment interface designed to simplify the integration of Elavon’s payment gateway into web applications. It provides a responsive, modal-based UI for capturing sensitive payment details without exposing your environment to PCI compliance risks. By leveraging the Lightbox, merchants can deliver a seamless checkout experience while maintaining security and compliance standards.
### Include the library
Add the script to your page:
```javascript
````
Use the URL for the environment you are currently using:
- Sandbox: `https://uat.hpp.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/client/index.js`
- Production: `https://hpp.na.elavonpayments.com/client/index.js`
## Core components and functions
---
### ElavonLightbox constructor
**Purpose**: Create and configure a lightbox instance with your options and callbacks.
**Key Configurations**:
`sessionId` (string): ID from your created payment session.
`messageHandler` (function): Callback to process messages from the lightbox.
`onReady` (function): Called when the component is fully loaded.
Documentation -> :magic-link[ElavonLightbox()]{id="elavonlightbox-constructor"}
---
### messageHandler function
**Purpose**: Intercept and handle lightbox messages (events) with custom logic, while preserving the built‑in flow by calling defaultAction().
**Use when**:
- Integrate custom business logic into the payment flow without breaking Elavon’s default behavior.
- Capture and process payment events.
Documentation -> :magic-link[messageHandler()]{id="messagehandler-function-lightbox"}
---
### defaultAction function
**Purpose**: Run the lightbox’s built‑in behavior for the current message type.
**Use when**:
- If you use custom logic in `messageHandler` to ensure the lightbox behaves as expected.
- Whenever you override or extend message handling but still want Elavon’s default actions.
Documentation -> :magic-link[defaultAction()]{id="defaultaction-function"}
---
### onReady function
**Purpose**: Hook that fires when the lightbox component is loaded and ready to be interacted with.
**Use when**:
- Verify successful initialization of the lightbox.
- Handle initialization errors gracefully.
Documentation -> :magic-link[onReady()]{id="onready-function-lightbox"}
---
### show function
**Purpose**: Programmatically open (display) the lightbox UI.
**Use when**:
- The lightbox has been successfully initialized.
Documentation -> :magic-link[show()]{id="show-function-lightbox"}
---
### hide function
**Purpose**: Programmatically close/dismiss the lightbox UI.
**Use when**:
- Close the lightbox overlay.
- Provide custom close button that triggers the lightbox to hide.
Documentation -> :magic-link[hide()]{id="hide-function-lightbox"}

_source.title: ElavonLightbox constructor
_source.genAiContent:
# elavon-payment-gateway
## ElavonLightbox constructor
The constructor creates a new instance of the `ElavonLightbox` class from the global window object and assigns it to the variable lightbox. The `ElavonLightbox` is a JavaScript class provided by the SDK library, used to initialize and manage a secure, embedded payment interface for processing transactions.
By instantiating it with new, you're preparing the lightbox component with necessary configuration so it can be displayed and interact with the user during the payment flow.
## Syntax
```javascript
lightbox = new window.ElavonLightbox(message)
```
## Parameters
| Parameter
|
Type
|
Description |
|----------|-------------|:------|
| message | :magic-link[[Constructor]]{id="elavonlightbox-constructor#constructor-message-object"} | **Required.** The constructor takes a single argument that is an object with the `sessionId`, `messageHandler()`, and optional `onReady()`. |
## Constructor message object
| Attributes
|
Type
|
Description |
|----------|-------------|:------|
| sessionId | string | **Required.** The session id obtained from creating a [/payment-session](/products/elavon-payment-gateway/v1/api-reference#tag/Payment-Sessions/Checkout-Experience) via the API. |
| messageHandler | :magic-link[[function]]{id="messagehandler-function-lightbox"} | **Required.** Takes two arguments: the message itself and a function that will run the built-in default action for a given message type.
| onReady | :magic-link[[function]]{id="onready-function-lightbox"} | Uses a single boolean argument to reflect if the lightbox encountered an error when loading.|
## Example usage
:::code-block
```javascript
let lightbox = new window.ElavonLightbox({
sessionId: sessionId,
onReady: (error) =>
error
? console.error('Lightbox failed to load')
: lightbox.show(),
messageHandler: (message, defaultAction) => {
switch (message.type) {
case MessageTypes.transactionCreated:
submitData({
sessionId: message.sessionId,
transaction: message.transaction,
authorized: message.isAuthorized
});
break;
case MessageTypes.error:
console.error('An error occurred:', message.error);
break;
}
defaultAction();
},
});
```

_source.title: messageHandler function
_source.genAiContent:
# elavon-payment-gateway
## messageHandler function
The `messageHandler` is a callback function, which is used to process incoming messages from the `ElavonLightbox` component. This handler allows you to implement custom logic based on the message type (like submitting data to your backend), while still preserving the lightbox’s built-in behavior by calling `defaultAction().`
## When to use messageHandler()
Use this function when you need to:
- Integrate custom business logic into the payment flow without breaking Elavon’s default behavior.
- Capture and process payment events.
## Syntax
```javascript
function messageHandler(message, defaultAction) {
// Custom logic based on message.type
defaultAction(); // Always call this to maintain default behavior
}
```
## Parameters
| Parameter
|
Type
|
Description |
|----------|-------------|:------|
| message | :magic-link[[Message]]{id="messagehandler-function-lightbox#message-object"} | Contains details about the lightbox state and event. |
| defaultAction | :magic-link[[function]]{id="defaultaction-function"} | Executes the built-in default action for the given message type. Always call this unless you intentionally override default behavior.
|
## Returns
The function does not return a value. However, the message object provides event details:
### Message object
| Attributes
|
Type
|
Description |
|----------|-------------|:------||
| type
| :magic-link[[Type]]{id="messagehandler-function-lightbox#type-map"} | Indicates the event type. Always compare against :magic-link[window.ElavonLightbox.MessageTypes]{id="messagehandler-function-lightbox#message-types"} map.
|
| sessionId | string | The session id obtained from creating a :magic-link[/payment-session]{id="/products/elavon-payment-gateway/v1/api-reference#tag/Payment-Sessions/operation/CreatePaymentSession"} |
| hostedCard | string | Hosted card ID created during the flow. |
| error | string | Error message if the process fails. |
### Type map
Always reference `window.ElavonLightbox.MessageTypes` directly to keep code working if these values are updated in the future. Do not use string values for `message.type`.
### Syntax
```javascript
const MessageTypes = window.ElavonLightbox.MessageTypes;
```
| Field
|
Type
|
Description |
|----------|-------------|:------|
| loaded | string | Component fully loaded and ready. |
| hostedCardCreated | string | Hosted payment card was successfully created. |
| closeOverlay | string | Indicates that a modal or lightbox UI element was closed.
|
| error | string | Error occurred during session or transaction. |
| methodSessionCompleted | string | Payment method session completed. |
| sessionCompleted | string | Payment flow completed.|
| transactionCreated | string | Transaction recorded successfully. |
## Example usage
:::code-block
```javascript
messageHandler: (message, defaultAction) => {
switch (message.type) {
case MessageTypes.transactionCreated:
submitData({
sessionId: message.sessionId
});
break;
case MessageTypes.hostedCardCreated:
submitData({
sessionId: message.sessionId,
hostedCardId: message.hostedCard,
});
break;
case MessageTypes.error:
console.error('An error occurred:', message.error);
break;
}
defaultAction();
}
:::

The `hide()` function is part of the lightbox library and is used to close or dismiss the currently active lightbox UI. This is typically called when you want to programmatically remove the lightbox from view without requiring user interaction.

const redirectToNew Page = () => {
