_source.title: Collect additional information
_source.genAiContent:
# elavon-payment-gateway
## Collect additional information
The payment page allows you to collect more than just payment details—it’s also where you gather important information like billing and shipping addresses, and the customer’s email. This helps ensure secure transactions, accurate deliveries, and timely communication about the order.
1. Log in to the :magic-link[merchant portal]{id="create-account#merchant-portal-web-based-interface"}.
2. Navigate to **Settings**, then go to the **Hosted payments page** section.
## Billing and shipping address
Billing information is used to verify the cardholder’s identity. Matching billing details with the payment method helps reduce fraudulent transactions and chargebacks.
:::::notice{note}
You can increase your chances of getting a frictionless 3-D Secure 2 experience by capturing Billing Address as part of a transaction. We highly recommend either capturing billing information prior to creating a payment session or including billing address fields in your Hosted Payments Form.
:::::
There are three sets of data fields that can be included on your hosted payment page: Payment Info, Billing Address, and Shipping Address.
These fields can be configured in three ways:
- Payment Info Only
- Payment Info and Billing Address
- Payment Info, Billing Address, Shipping Address
When included, billing and shipping address fields are required for processing a payment.
## Email address data capture
Merchants can choose to email sales and refund receipts using an Elavon Payment Gateway-generated receipt. This setting is controlled from the **Receipt Settings** page.
If you to send sales and/or refund receipts through Elavon Payment Gateway but don't capture and pass the email address prior to initiating the payment session, then a section to capture this information is appended to the bottom of the payment form.
## Pre-populate payment page
If you know your shopper's name, billing and shipping info, you can pass that information to the payment page so they don't have to enter it again. Any billing and/or shipping information captured in a [/payment-session](/products/elavon-payment-gateway/v1/api-reference#tag/Payment-Sessions/operation/CreatePaymentSession) or in [/orders](/products/elavon-payment-gateway/v1/api-reference#tag/Orders/operation/CreateOrder) will populate those fields on the form. Customers are still able to add/edit any pre-populated fields, if desired.
**Example payment form with pre-populated info**
:::image{id="/content/dam/developer-portal-solutions/en-gb/elavon/media/images/products/elavon-payment-gateway/prepopulated-address.png" alt="payment form with prepopulated shipping and billing address" max-width="352" max-height="542"}
:::
**Example server.js file payment session function**
:::code
```javascript
app.get('/payment-session', async (req, res) => {
const orderID = await createOrder();
const requestBody = {
hppType: "fullPageRedirect",
returnUrl: "http://localhost:3000/return.html",
cancelUrl: "http://localhost:3000/cancel.html",
order: orderID,
doCreateTransaction: "true",
shopperEmailAddress: "jennifer.court@email.com",
billTo: {
fullName: "Jennifer Court",
street1: "123 Test Street",
street2: "Suite B",
city: "London",
region: "England",
postalCode: "NW1 6XE",
countryCode: "GBR"
},
shipTo: {
street1: "Round About Way",
street2: "Suite B",
city: "London",
region: "England",
postalCode: "NW1 6XE",
countryCode: "GBR"
}
};
const paymentSessionRequest = {
method: 'post',
url: 'https://dev1.api.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/payment-sessions',
headers: {
'Content-Type': 'application/json',
'Authorization': `Basic ${encoded}`
},
data: requestBody
};
const response = await axios(paymentSessionRequest);
res.json(response.data.url);
});
```
:::

_source.title: Wallets
_source.genAiContent:
# elavon-payment-gateway
## Wallets
To use digital wallets on the hosted payment page, you'll need to go to the **Payments Settings** page of the EPG merchant portal.
1. Log in to the :magic-link[EPG merchant portal]{id="create-account#merchant-portal-web-based-interface"}.
2. Navigate to **Settings**, then go to the **Payments** section.
## Apple Pay
Apple Pay is a secure, payment system developed by Apple that allows shoppers to make purchases from the hosted payment page. It works by storing shopper's credit, debit, or prepaid cards in the Wallet app and using tokenization to protect your actual card details during transactions.
By enabling Apple Pay, you agree that you have read and consented to the [Apple Pay Web Merchant Terms and Conditions](https://developer.apple.com/apple-pay/terms/apple-pay-web).
:::::notice{Note}
To use Apple Pay in hosted payment lightbox, you must contact **support** while logged in to the merchant portal to register your domain with Apple. This only needs to be done once and not for each account.
:::::
## Google Pay
Google Pay offers a fast, secure, and frictionless payment option that eliminates the need for customers to manually enter card details. Plus, Google Pay uses advanced encryption and tokenization, giving shoppers confidence that their payment information is protected. Supporting Google Pay also opens your checkout to millions of Android and Chrome users who prefer digital wallets, helping you stay competitive and meet modern payment expectations.
By enabling Google Pay, you agree that you have read and consented to the [Google Pay API Terms and Conditions](https://payments.developers.google.com/terms/sellertos).
## Paze
Paze is a digital wallet and secure online checkout solution. It offers a secure, convenient, and seamless way to shop online using existing bank’s cards. Participating banks automatically adds eligible cards to a shopper's Paze wallet—no need to manually enter card details or create a new account. At checkout the shopper will have to option to make their payment with their Paze wallet.
To enable Paze as a payment option, contact **support** while logged in to the merchant portal.

_source.title: Overview
_source.genAiContent:
# elavon-payment-gateway
## Fields JS library
The Elavon Hosted Fields JavaScript library provides pre-written, reusable components to securely embed payment fields on your website. These fields are rendered inside iframes served by Elavon, ensuring sensitive cardholder data never touches your servers, reducing PCI compliance scope.
By using this library, you can:
- Embed secure payment fields without building custom iframe logic.
- Apply custom styling for a seamless checkout experience.
- Handle real-time validation and transaction events with built-in callbacks.
## Include the library
Add the script to your page:
```javascript
```
Use the URL for the environment you are currently using:
- Sandbox: `https://uat.hpp.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/hosted-fields-client/index.js`
- Production: `https://hpp.na.elavonpayments.com/hosted-fields-client/index.js`
## Core components and functions
---
### ElavonHostedFields constructor
**Purpose**: Initializes hosted fields and embeds secure iframes for card data entry.
**Key configurations**:
`sessionId` – Payment session ID from API.
`fields` – Field layout and wrapper IDs.
`styles` – CSS rules for iframe content.
`onReady()` – Callback when fields are loaded.
`onTransactionSubmission()` - Optional callback triggered before hosted field create a transaction.
`messageHandler()` – Callback for field and transaction events.
`hideUntilRequested` – Hide fields until show() is called.
Documentation -> :magic-link[ElavonHostedFields()]{id="elavonhostedfields-function"}
---
### messageHandler function
**Purpose**: Handles real-time field updates and transaction results.
**Triggered when**:
- Field state changes (focus, validity, value).
- Payment submission completes.
Common message types:
`fieldValidityChanged`, `fieldFocusChanged`, `transactionCreated`, `error`.
Documentation -> :magic-link[messageHandler()]{id="messagehandler-function"}
---
### getState function
**Purpose**: Retrieves current validation and readiness status of all fields.
**Use when**:
- Check if all fields are valid before enabling submit.
- Apply dynamic styling based on field state.
Documentation -> :magic-link[getState()]{id="getstate-function"}
---
### onReady function
**Purpose**: Fires when all hosted fields are fully loaded.
**Use when**:
- Show payment when fields are ready.
- Enable submit button.
- Apply initial styling safely.
Documentation -> :magic-link[onReady()]{id="onready-function-lightbox"}
---
### onTransactionSubmission function
**Purpose**: Executes custom logic before a transaction is created by hosted field
**Triggered when**:
- A transaction is about to be created through hosted fields (`doCreateTransaction = true`).
- The callback receives transaction details, including the `paymentMethodOrigin`
**Use when**:
- Logging or tracking the payment method used for the transaction.
- Running validation or preparation logic before submission.
- Performing conditional handling based on the payment method.
- Waiting for a process to complete before continuing the transactions.
Documentation -> :magic-link[onTrasactionSubmission()]{id="onTransactionSubmission-function"}
---
### addClass function
**Purpose**: Adds CSS class to a hosted field wrapper.
**Use when**: Highlight invalid fields or active states.
Documentation -> :magic-link[addClass()]{id="addclass-function"}
---
### fieldFocus function
**Purpose**: Move the focus to a specific field.
**Use when**: Guide shopper directly to the field that requires correction.
Documentation -> :magic-link[fieldFocus()]{id="focusfield-function"}
---
### removeClass function
**Purpose**: Removes CSS class from a hosted field iframe wrapper.
**Use when**: Clear error or highlight styles.
Documentation -> :magic-link[removeClass()]{id="removeclass-function"}
---
### setAttribute function
**Purpose**: Sets attributes (e.g., placeholder, ariaLabel) inside hosted field iframes.
**Use when**:
- Customize placeholder text.
- Improve accessibility dynamically.
- Setting a class name.
- Disabling a field
- Requiring a field
Documentation -> :magic-link[setAttribute()]{id="setattribute-function"}
---
### submit function
**Purpose**: Submits payment fields for processing.
**Use when**: Shopper clicks “Pay” button.
**Result**: Triggers `messageHandler()` with transaction details.
Documentation -> :magic-link[submit()]{id="submit-function"}
---
### updateSession function
**Purpose**: Updates the payment session details without reloading fields.
**Use when**: Session changes mid-checkout (e.g., updated amount).
Documentation -> :magic-link[updateSession()]{id="updatesession-function"}
---
### destroy function
**Purpose**: Removes hosted fields from DOM and cleans up resources.
**Use when**: Navigating away or resetting the form.
Documentation -> :magic-link[destroy()]{id="destroy-function"}
---
### show function
**Purpose**: Displays hosted fields if `hideUntilRequested` was set to `true`.
**Use when**: After `onReady()` fires and you want to reveal the form.
Documentation -> :magic-link[show()]{id="show-function-fields"}
---
### onSurchargeAcknowledgementRequired function
**Purpose**:
Display a prompt to capture consent before proceeding with a surcharge transaction.
**Use when**: If you are using surcharging this function is required to capture cardholder consent.
Documentation -> :magic-link[onSurchargeAcknowledgementRequired()]{id="onsurchargeacknowledgementrequired-function"}

# elavon-payment-gateway
## ElavonHostedFields constructor
The `ElavonHostedFields` constructor initializes the hosted payment fields on your website. It securely embeds input fields for sensitive card data (e.g., card number, CVV) inside iframes served by Elavon, ensuring PCI compliance and reducing your security burden. These fields are isolated from your DOM, so sensitive data never touches your servers.
## Syntax
```javascript
elavonHostedFields = new window.ElavonHostedFields(message);okay
```
## Parameters
| Parameter
|
Type
|
Description |
|----------|-------------|:------|
| message | :magic-link[[Constructor]]{id="elavonhostedfields-function#constructor-message-object"} | **Required.** The message configuration object creates an`` for each Elavon hosted field defined in the `fields` object, provides a `styles` object to style the iframes and calls the `messageHandler` and `onReady` functions to determine the state of the hosted fields. |
### Constructor message object
The constructor creates a new instance of the `ElavonHostedFields` class from the global window object and assigns it to the variable `elavonHostedFields`. The `ElavonHostedFields` is a JavaScript class provided by the SDK library, used to initialize and manage a secure, embedded payment fields for processing transactions.
By instantiating it with new, you're preparing the field components with necessary configuration so it can be displayed and interact with the user during the payment flow.
| Attributes
|
Type
|
Description |
|----------|-------------|:------|
| sessionId | string | **Required.** The session id obtained from :magic-link[/payment-session]{id="/products/elavon-payment-gateway/v1/api-reference#tag/Payment-Sessions/operation/CreatePaymentSession"}. |
| fields | :magic-link[[Fields]]{id="elavonhostedfields-function#fields-object"} | **Required.** An object containing one or more field configurations. Each containing information on fields, where to display them, and how to style them. The object :magic-link[fieldTypes]{id="addclass-function"} in the addClass function contains the available fields if they need to be retrieved at runtime. |
| styles | :magic-link[[Styles]]{id="elavonhostedfields-function#styles-object"} | CSS styling overrides and additional CSS rules to be included in every iframe. If you pass CSS into styles in the `styles` object, it is applied to every iframe. If you pass CSS into the `fields` object, it will only apply to that single iframe.
|
| onReady | :magic-link[[function]]{id="onready-function#onready-funtion"}
| Callback function called when all hosted fields are loaded into the form. |
| messageHandler | :magic-link[[function]]{id="messagehandler-function#messagehandler-function"}
| Callback function the hosted fields library will use to communicate field status.
|
| onSurchargeAcknowledgementRequired | :magic-link[[function]]{id="onsurchargeacknowledgementrequired-function"} | If using surcharging this function is **required** to meet the strict compliance requirements to disclose the surcharge amounts clearly.
|
| hideUntilRequested | boolean | If true, the hosted payment fields will not be rendered until you call the `show()` function. Note that show function will not be functional until after the `onReady()` is called. |
| onTransactionSubmission
| :magic-link[[function]]{id="onTransactionSubmission-function"} | If provided, this function will be called before the hosted fields creates a transaction to allow performing custom logic before proceeding with the transaction.
|
### Fields object
A configuration object that specifies the UI schema for your payment page. It determines which form fields (e.g., card number, expiration date, billing address) are rendered and which alternative payment options (e.g., PayPal, Apple Pay, Google Pay) are exposed (e.g., fields.cardholderName):
| Attributes
|
Type
|
Description |
|----------|-------------|:------|
| cardholderName | :magic-link[[fieldConfig]]{id="elavonhostedfields-function#field-configuration-object"} | Input field for the name of the cardholder as it appears on the card.
|
| cardNumber | :magic-link[[fieldConfig]]{id="elavonhostedfields-function#field-configuration-object"} | **Required.** Input field for the credit or debit card number. Accepts numeric values only and typically formatted in groups of four digits (e.g., 1234 5678 9012 3456). |
| cardExpirationDate | :magic-link[[fieldConfig]]{id="elavonhostedfields-function#field-configuration-object"} | **Required.** Input field for the card’s expiration date. Formatted as MM/YY and used to verify the card’s validity period. |
| cardCvv | :magic-link[[fieldConfig]]{id="elavonhostedfields-function#field-configuration-object"} |Input field for the card’s CVV (Card Verification Value). A 3- or 4-digit security code found on the back or front of the card, used for authentication. |
| googlePayButton | :magic-link[[fieldConfig Google]]{id="elavonhostedfields-function#field-configuration-object-for-googlepaybutton"} | Button that initiates payment using Google Pay. Displays the Google Pay logo and triggers the native Google Pay flow.|
| applePayButton | :magic-link[[fieldConfig Apple]]{id="elavonhostedfields-function#field-configuration-object-for-applepaybutton"} | Button that initiates payment using Apple Pay. Displays the Apple Pay logo and triggers the native Apple Pay flow. |
| pazeButton | :magic-link[[fieldConfig Paze]]{id="elavonhostedfields-function#field-configuration-object-for-pazebutton"} | Button that initiates payment using Paze. Displays the Paze logo and triggers the native Paze flow. |
### Field configuration object
Each hosted field supports several configuration properties that control its rendering and behavior. These properties allow you to customize the UI, accessibility, and behavior of each payment field (e.g., fields.cardNumber.placeholder):.
| Properties
|
Type
|
Description |
|----------|-------------|:------|
| wrapperId | string| **Required.** The id of a DOM element where the fields should be inserted. It does NOT matter what type of element it is, as long as it can contain children. For instance, div, span, form, etc. Each field does not necessarily need its own unique container. You can insert all of the fields into a shared container.
|
| label | string | If provided, the hosted fields library will include an HTML label element within the iframe with the input. If you wish for a label to be rendered outside of the iframe, do not include this attribute. |
| className | string | A css class from your stylesheet to style the element the iframe will go into. Ensure this style is included in the `` of your document or a rel link to a .css file that contains it. To style the contents inside the iframe, see the `styles` object. |
| ariaLabel | string
| The aria-label is an accessibility attribute used in HTML to provide a textual description for an element, especially when that element doesn't have visible text that screen readers can use. Defaults to generic label i.e. Cardholder Name. |
| placeholder| string
| Placeholder text provides a hint or example of the expected input format.|
| required | string
| Marks the field as required. |
| disabled | string | Disables the field from being click on. |
### Field configuration object for googlePayButton
To use Google Pay you need to enable the digital wallet from the Payments Settings page of the :magic-link[merchant portal]{id="create-account#merchant-portal-web-based-interface"}.
If using the Google Pay Button, be sure you are following [Google's brand guidelines](https://developers.google.com/pay/api/web/guides/brand-guidelines). See [button options supported by Google](https://developers.google.com/pay/api/web/reference/request-objects#ButtonOptions) for a full list.
By enabling Google Pay, you agree that you have read and consented to the [Google Pay API Terms and Conditions](https://payments.developers.google.com/terms/sellertos).
| Properties
|
Type
|
Description |
|----------|-------------|:------|
| wrapperId |string | **Required.** The id of a DOM element where the button should be inserted.
|
| captureBilingAddress | boolean | Determines whether you want to capture billing address from Google Pay. |
| captureShippingAddress | boolean | Determines whether you want to capture shipping address from Google Pay. |
| buttonColor | string | **default:** A Google-selected default value. Currently black but it may change over time (default).
**black:** A black button suitable for use on white or light backgrounds.
**white:** A white button suitable for use on colorful backgrounds.
|
| buttonType | string | **book:** The "Book with Google Pay" payment button.
**buy:** The "Buy with Google Pay" payment button.
**checkout:** The "Checkout with Google Pay" payment button.
**donate:** The "Donate with Google Pay" payment button.
**order:** The "Order with Google Pay" payment button.
**pay:** The "Pay with Google Pay" payment button.
**plain:** The Google Pay payment button without the additional text.
**subscribe:** The "Subscribe with Google Pay" payment button.
|
| buttonSizeMode | string | **static:** Button has a static width and height (default).
**fill:** Button size changes to fill the size of its container. |
| buttonLocale | string | The ISO 639-1 code represents the desired button language. Supported locales include en, ar, bg, ca, cs, da, de, el, es, et, fi, fr, hr, id, it, ja, ko, ms, nl, no, pl, pt, ru, sk, sl, sr, sv, th, tr, uk, and zh.
The default is set to the browser or operating system language settings. |
### Field configuration object for applePayButton
To use Apple Pay you need to enable the digital wallet from the Payments Settings page of the :magic-link[merchant portal]{id="create-account#merchant-portal-web-based-interface"}.
If using the ApplePay Button, be sure you are following [Apple's brand guidelines](https://developer.apple.com/design/human-interface-guidelines/apple-pay).
By enabling Apple Pay, you agree that you have read and consented to the [Apple Pay Web Merchant Terms and Conditions](https://developer.apple.com/apple-pay/terms/apple-pay-web).
| Properties
|
Type
|
Description |
|----------|-------------|:------|
| wrapperId | string | **Required.** The id of a DOM element where the button should be inserted.
|
| registeredDomain | string |
**Required.** Your registered Apply Pay domain. |
| captureBillingAddress | boolean | Determines whether you want to capture billing address from Apple Pay. |
| captureShippingAddress |boolean |
Determines whether you want to capture shipping address from Apple Pay. |
| buttonStyle | string | **black:** A black button with white lettering.
**white:** A white button with black lettering.
**white-outline:** A white button with black lettering and a black outline.
|
| buttonType | string | **book:** Button useful for booking trips, flights, or other experiences.
**buy:** Button useful for product purchases.
**check-out:** Button useful for purchase experiences that include other payment buttons that start with “Check out.”
**continue:** Button useful for general purchases.
**contribute:** Button useful to help people contribute money to projects, causes, organizations, and other entities.
**donate:**
Button used by approved nonprofit organization that lets people make donations.
**order:** Button useful for placing orders for items such as meals or flowers.
**pay:** Button useful for paying bills or invoices.
**plain:** Button with the Apple Pay logo only, useful when an additional call to action isn’t needed.
**reload:** Button useful for adding money to a card, account, or payment system.
**rent:** Button useful for renting items such as cars or scooters.
**set-up:** Button useful for prompting the user to set up a card.
**subscribe:** Button useful for purchasing a subscription such as a gym membership or meal-kit delivery service.
**support:** Button useful for helping people give money to projects, causes, organizations, and other entities.
|
| buttonLocale | string |
The button locale type uses ISO-639-1 code for the language, followed by a hyphen (-), and the ISO-3166-1 code for the region. Supported locales include ar-AB, ca-ES, cs-CZ, da-DK, de-DE, el-GR, en-AU, en-GB, en-US, es-ES, es-MX, fi-FI, fr-CA, fr-FR, he-IL, hi-IN, hr-HR, hu-HU, id-ID, it-IT, ja-JP, ko-KR, ms-MY, nb-NO, nl-NL, pl-PL, pt-BR, pt-PT, ro-RO, ru-RU, sk-SK, sv-SE, th-TH, tr-TR, uk-UA, vi-VN,zh-CN, zh-HK, and zh-TW. |
### Field configuration object for pazeButton
To use Paze you need to enable the digital wallet from the Payments Settings page of the :magic-link[merchant portal]{id="create-account#merchant-portal-web-based-interface"}.
Billing address will be captured and included on the transaction through the Paze flow. Paze does not currently support capturing a shipping address. If needed, shipping address can be captured elsewhere and included in the payment session before displaying the Paze button.
| Properties
|
Type
|
Description |
|----------|-------------|:------|
| wrapperId |string | **Required.** The id of a DOM element where the button should be inserted.
|
### Styles object
Use the style object to style elements inside of the Elavon hosted iframe.
| Attributes
|
Type
|
Description |
|----------|-------------|:------|
| input | object | A css class. |
| style | string | The styling to be applied to the css class. |
## Example usage
:::code-block
```javascript
elavonHostedFields = new window.ElavonHostedFields({
sessionId: sessionId,
fields: {
cardholderName: {
wrapperId: 'cardholder-name',
className: 'field-height', //Use to style element the iframe will render in
},
cardNumber: {
wrapperId: 'card-number',
className: 'field-height',
},
cardExpirationDate: {
wrapperId: 'card-exp-date',
className: 'field-height',
placeholder: 'MM/YY'
},
cardCvv: {
wrapperId: 'card-cvv',
className: 'field-height',
placeholder: 'e.g. 123'
},
applePayButton: {
wrapperId: 'apple-pay-button',
registeredDomain: 'my-registered-domain.com'
}
},
styles: { //Use to style element inside the iframe
'input': {
'width': '100%',
'padding': '10px',
'font-size': '16px',
'border': '1px solid #ccc',
'border-radius': '4px',
'box-sizing': 'border-box',
},
'input:hover': {
'color': 'blue',
},
'.field-error': {
'border': '1px solid #d32f2f',
}
},
onReady: onReady,
messageHandler: messageHandler,
hideUntilRequested: false
});
```
:::

_source.title: messageHandler function
_source.genAiContent:
# elavon-payment-gateway
## messageHandler function
The `messageHandler()` callback is invoked by the Elavon Hosted Fields library whenever:
- A hosted payment field’s state changes (e.g., focus, validity, value).
- A payment submission occurs and transaction details are available.
This function is essential for real-time UI updates, error handling, and communicating payment outcomes to the shopper.
**When to use messageHandler()**
Use this function when you need to:
- Detect changes in validity, focus, and value to provide immediate feedback.
- Display error messages when integration or validation issues occur.
- Inform the shopper about authorization results and next steps.
## Syntax
```javascript
function messageHandler(message) {
// Handle field updates or transaction results
}
```
## Parameters
| Parameter
|
Type
| Description |
|----------|-------------|:------|
| message | object | **Required.** Callback function the `ElavonHostedField()` will use to communicate pertinent information about the status of the fields and payments. |
## Returns
### Field update
The following object will be returned anytime a field is updated by page loading or shopper interaction. The information returned can be used to communicate to the shopper regarding field validation and error messaging (e.g., message.isValid):
| Properties
|
Type
| Description |
|----------|-------------|:------|
| instanceId | string | The ID number for this instance. |
| fieldType | string | The field responsible for this message.
Possible values:
`cardholderName`
`cardNumber`
`cardExpirationDate`
`cardCvv`
`googlePayButton`
`applePayButton`
`pazeButton`|
| type | string | The message type. Depending on the type, additional message attributes may be included.
Possible values:
**error:** returned when an error occurs in the integration. Fields `code` and `message` will also be returned.
**fieldLoaded:** the hosted field was added to the DOM and is ready to use.
**fieldFocusChanged:** the input focus was changed. Field `isFocused` will also be returned.
**fieldValidityChanged:** the field either became valid or invalid. Fields `isValid` and `error` will also be returned.
**fieldValueChanged:** the value of the input changed. Field `isEmpty` will also be returned.
**surchargeAdviceUpdated:** the card information was changed and surcharge information is available.
|
| code | string| If `type = error` returns the error message.
**invalidPaymentSession:** the session id was either not provided, invalid, or has already been consumed.
**unexpectedLoadingError:** an unexpected error occurred when trying to load the payment fields. This should be reported to our support team to investigate. A remediation may require creating a new payment session and a new ElavonHostedFields instance.
**unexpectedSubmitError:** an unexpected error occurred when trying to submit the payment. This should be reported to our support team to investigate. Direct the shopper to try again or try another payment method.
**cardVerificationFailed:** 3D Secure verification failed. Direct the shopper to try a different payment method. |
| isFocused | boolean | If `type = fieldFocusChanged` indicates if field is focused or unfocused. |
| isValid | boolean | If `type = fieldValidityChanged` indicated if field has valid or invalid data. |
| error | string | Any error messages returned. |
| isEmpty | boolean| If `type = fieldValueChanged` indicated whether or not field is currently empty.
| wrapperId | string | The wrapperID value provided in the `ElavonHostedFields()` constructor. |
### Transaction information
When the :magic-link[submit()]{id="submit-function"} function is called the `messageHandler()` will return the following object. The information returned will help you direct the shopper what to do depending on the status of the transaction (e.g., message.isAuthorized):
| Properties
|
Type
| Description |
|----------|-------------|:------|
| instanceId | string | The ID number for this instance. |
| transaction | :magic-link[[Transaction]]{id="messagehandler-function#transaction-object"} | Contains information about the transaction. |
| isAuthorized | boolean | Indicates if the transaction was authorized. If false, the transaction was declined and you should communicate that the shopper should try again or use a different payment method. |
| canRetry | boolean | Indicates if another transaction can be attempted using the same payment session. After a decline, the payment session supports retrying up to 8 times. |
| type
| string| Possible values:
**hostedCardCreated:** if `doCreateTransaction` is false on the payment session, a hosted card will be created on submission of the fields and this event will be sent upon completion
**transactionCreated:** if `doCreateTransaction` is true on the payment session, this message will be sent upon creation of the transaction|
### Transaction object
The transaction object represents a comprehensive record of a payment event, capturing key details about the payment method, shopper interaction, and processing outcomes (e.g., message.transaction.state):
| Properties
|
Type
| Description |
|----------|-------------|:------|
| card | object | Card details used for the transaction.
Includes:
:magic-link[`billTo`]{id="submit-function#object-properties"},
:magic-link[`shipTo`]{id="submit-function#object-properties"}, `brand`, `expirationMonth`, `expirationYear`, `holderName`, `last4`, `scheme`|
| createdAt | string| Timestamp when the transaction record was created. |
| credentialOnFileType | string| Possible values:
`subscription`
`recurring`
`unscheduled` |
| doCapture | boolean| If false, authorize only; if true (default), authorize and capture funds for settlement. |
| doSendReceipt | boolean | Send receipt to shopper's email address. Default value is based on account configuration. |
| failures | array | Failure details, if the transaction was not authorized. |
| id | string | Unique identifier for the transaction generated.
|
| isAuthorized | boolean | Indicates whether the authorization succeeded. |
| isHeldForReview | boolean| Indicates whether the transaction is flagged for manual review (e.g., by fraud rules). |
| paymentMethodOrigin | string | The origin of the payment method. |
| paymentSession | string | Payment session url. |
| shopperEmailAddress | string| Shopper's email address. |
| shopperInteraction | string | The type of interaction with the shopper that generated this payment session.
Possible values:
`ecommerce`
`mailOrder`
`telephoneOrder`
`merchantInitiated`
`inPerson` |
| state |string | Possible values:
`declined`
`authorized`
`captured`
`voided`
`settled`
`expired`
`settlementDelayed`
`rejected`
`heldForReview`
`unknown`
`authorizationPending`
|
| source | string | Will indicate the source as hosted payment fields `hppFields` |
| total | object | Contains information about the transaction total. |
## Example usage
:::code-block
```javascript
function messageHandler(message) {
if(message.fieldType === "cardholderName" && message.isFocused === true) {
elavonHostedFields.removeClass(fieldTypes.cardholderName, 'field-error');
}
if(message.fieldType === "cardNumber" && message.isFocused === true) {
elavonHostedFields.removeClass(fieldTypes.cardNumber, 'field-error');
}
if(message.fieldType === "cardExpirationDate" && message.isFocused === true) {
elavonHostedFields.removeClass(fieldTypes.cardExpirationDate, 'field-error');
}
if(message.fieldType === "cardCvv" && message.isFocused === true) {
elavonHostedFields.removeClass(fieldTypes.cardCvv, 'field-error');
}
if(message.type === "transactionCreated" ){
if(message.isAuthorized) {
window.location = 'return.html';
}
else {
alert('Card declined.');
}
}
}
```
:::

_source.title: getState function
_source.genAiContent:
# elavon-payment-gateway
## getState function
The `getState()` function retrieves the real-time validation and readiness status of all Elavon Hosted Fields. It provides a snapshot of the form state, including whether fields are empty, valid, focused, or loaded. This is essential for controlling UI feedback, enabling/disabling the submit button, and preventing incomplete or invalid submissions.
**Why use getState()**
-
Check if all fields are valid before enabling the submit button.
- Apply or remove error classes based on per-field validity.
- Show real-time feedback (e.g., highlight invalid fields).
- Prevent submission until all required fields are ready and valid.
## Syntax
```javascript
const state = elavonHostedFields.getState();
```
## Returns
The function returns a state object with the following properties:
| Properties
|
Type
| Description |
|----------|-------------|:------|
| fields | :magic-link[[Fields]]{id="getstate-function#fields-object"} | Contains per-field status objects for `cardNumber`, `cardholderName`, `cardExpirationDate`, and `cardCvv`. |
| hidden | boolean | Indicates if the hosted fields container is currently hidden. |
| isValid | boolean | True if all input fields have valid data; false otherwise. |
### Fields object
Each field (e.g., state.fields.cardNumber.isValid) includes:
| Properties
|
Type
| Description |
|----------|-------------|:------|
| error | string | Current validation error message, if any. |
| isEmpty | boolean | true if the field has no value entered. |
| isFocused | boolean | true if the field currently has focus. |
| isLoaded | boolean | true if the field iframe is fully loaded and ready.|
| isTouched | boolean
| true if the shopper has interacted with the field. |
| isValid | boolean | true if the field’s value passes validation. |
| wrapperId | boolean | The wrapper ID provided during initialization. |
## Example usage
Example showing a basic validation check and adding an error class:
:::code-block
```javascript
const state = elavonHostedFields.getState();
if(!state.isValid){
if(!state.fields.cardholderName.isValid) {
elavonHostedFields.addClass(fieldTypes.cardholderName, 'field-error');
}
return;
}
```
:::

_source.title: onReady function
_source.genAiContent:
# elavon-payment-gateway
## onReady function
The `onReady()` callback is triggered when Elavon Hosted Fields have finished loading and are fully initialized. This ensures that any actions requiring the hosted fields—such as displaying them, enabling form submission, or applying dynamic styling—are only executed once the fields are ready. Using `onReady()` prevents issues like incomplete rendering, broken UI, or errors caused by interacting with fields before they are available.
**When to use onReady()**
Use this function when you need to:
- Ensures hosted fields are fully loaded before interaction.
- Prevent shoppers from seeing partially rendered or non-functional fields.
- To safely apply styling, event listeners, or show the payment form.
## Syntax
```javascript
function onReady()
```
## Example usage
Here’s a simple example of showing the hosted fields when they are ready:
```javascript
function onReady() {
elavonHostedFields.show();
}
```

_source.title: onTransactionSubmission function
_source.genAiContent:
# elavon-payment-gateway
## onTransactionSubmission function
`onTransactionSubmission()` is a callback function that is triggered before the hosted fields create a transaction, which allows you to run custom logic before the transaction is submitted.
The callback provides transaction details, including the `paymentMethodOrigin` for applying custom logic based on the payment method. The transaction proceeds only after the `proceed` function is called.
**When to use onTransactionSubmission() function**
Use this function when you need to execute custom logic immediately before a transaction is created through hosted fields.
Common use cases include:
* Logging or tracking transaction submission events.
* Running validation or preparation logic before the transaction proceeds.
* Performing conditional processing based on the paymentMethodOrigin.
* Waiting for an asynchronous process to complete before continuing with the transactions.
## Syntax
```javascript
onTransactionSubmission (details, proceed)
```
## Parameters
| Parameter
|
Type
| Description |
|----------|-------------|:------|
| `details` |
:magic-link[[details object]]{id="onTransactionSubmission-function#details-object"}| Provides information about transaction being submitted, including the `paymentMethodOrigins` property, which indicates the payment method used. |
| `proceed` | function | A function that must be called to continue with transaction creation. This allows you to delay submission until custom logic or asynchronous operations are completed. |
:::::notice{Important}
This function should not be used to display information to the customer. For digital wallet payments, it may be triggered while the wallet’s payment window is still open, and most wallets do not permit any content to overlay their interface.
:::::
### details object
This contains information about the transaction that will be created.
| Properties
|
Type
| Description |
|----------|-------------|:------|
| `paymentMethodOrigin` | string | It describes which payment method the user interacted with on the form.
Possible values:
**Card:** Transaction initiated using a standard payment card entered through hosted field.
**Apple Pay:**
Transaction initiated using Apple pay.
**Google Pay:**
Transaction initiated using Google pay.
**Paze:** Transaction initiated using Paze digital wallet. |
:::::notice{Note}
`onTransactionSubmission()`
is only triggered when
`doCreateTransaction` is set to true. Otherwise, the callback is not executed because the hosted fields do not create the transaction.
:::::
## Example usage
:::code-block
```javascript
function onTransactionSubmission(details, proceed) {
logPaymentMethodDetails(details).then(() => {
proceed();
});
}
async function logPaymentMethodDetails(details) {
if (details.paymentMethodOrigin === 'Card') {
//Your custom logic goes here
}
}
```
:::

_source.title: addClass function
_source.genAiContent:
# elavon-payment-gateway
## addClass function
The `addClass()` function allows you to dynamically add CSS classes to the secure iframe fields used for payment data entry. This is commonly used to visually indicate validation errors, highlight active fields, or apply custom styling based on shopper interactions.
**When to use addClass()**
Use this function when you need to:
- Apply an error class when a field fails validation.
- Add a visual cue when a field is focused or selected.
- Dynamically change the appearance of fields based on user actions or form state.
You can use :magic-link[`getState()`]{id="getstate-function"} to determine which fields are currently invalid.
## Syntax
```javascript
const fieldTypes = window.ElavonHostedFields.FieldTypes;
elavonHostedFields.addClass(fieldTypes.fieldName, 'className');
```
## Parameters
| Parameter
|
Type
| Description |
|----------|-------------|:------|
| fieldTypes | :magic-link[[fieldTypes]]{id="focusfield-function#fieldtypes-object"} | **Required.**
The specific field type to apply the CSS class to. Use one of the properties from FieldTypes (e.g., fieldTypes.cardNumber). |
| className | string | **Required.**
The name of the CSS class to add. This class should be defined in the styles object when initializing :magic-link[ElavonHostedFields]{id="elavonhostedfields-function#constructor-message-object"} |
### fieldTypes object
The FieldTypes object defines the available hosted fields you can target (e.g., fieldTypes.cardNumber):
| Properties
|
Type
| Description |
|----------|-------------|:------|
| cardNumber | string | Represents the card number input field (PAN). |
| cardholderName | string | Represents the cardholder name input field. |
| cardCvv | string | Represents the CVV (security code) input field. |
| cardExpirationDate | string | Represents the card expiration date input field. |
## Example usage
Add an error class when a field is invalid:
:::code-block
```javascript
const fieldTypes = window.ElavonHostedFields.FieldTypes;
const state = elavonHostedFields.getState();
if(!state.fields.cardholderName.isValid) {
elavonHostedFields.addClass(fieldTypes.cardholderName, 'field-error');
}
```
:::

_source.title: focusField function
_source.genAiContent:
# elavon-payment-gateway
## focusField function
This `focusField` function allows you to programmatically set focus to a specific hosted field. This function is most used during form validation to guide users directly to the field that requires correction or completion. This improves the overall user experience by ensuring users immediately see which field requires attention.
By explicitly setting focus, you help users immediately understand where action is required, reducing confusion and improving checkout or data-entry completion rates.
Guide users directly to the field that requires correction or completion.
**When to use focusField()**
Use this function when you need to:
- Guide shopper directly to the field that requires correction.
- Guide shopper to empty fields.
You can use :magic-link[`getState()`]{id="getstate-function"} to determine which fields are currently invalid.
## Syntax
```javascript
const fieldTypes = window.ElavonHostedFields.FieldTypes;
elavonHostedFields.focusField(fieldTypes.fieldName);
```
:::::notice{Note}
- Ensure Elavon hosted fields are fully initialized before calling this function.
- Use together with the `getstate` function to detect invalid fields and set focus accordingly.
- Only one field can be focused at a time.
:::::
## Parameters
| Parameter
|
Type
| Description |
|----------|-------------|:------|
| `fieldTypes` | :magic-link[[fieldTypes]]{id="addclass-function#fieldtypes-object"} | **Required.**
The specific field type to move focus to. Use one of the properties from `fieldTypes` (e.g., fieldTypes.cardNumber). |
### fieldTypes object
The `fieldTypes` object defines the available hosted fields you can target (e.g., fieldTypes.cardNumber):
| Properties
|
Type
| Description |
|----------|-------------|:------|
| `cardNumber` | string | Represents the card number input field (PAN). |
| `cardholderName` | string | Represents the cardholder name input field. |
| `cardCvv` | string | Represents the CVV (security code) input field. |
| `cardExpirationDate` | string | Represents the card expiration date input field. |
## Example usage
Moving focus to a field that is invalid:
:::code-block
```javascript
const fieldTypes = window.ElavonHostedFields.FieldTypes;
const state = elavonHostedFields.getState();
if(!state.fields.cardNumber.isValid) {
elavonHostedFields.focusField(fieldTypes.cardNumber);
}
```
:::

_source.title: removeClass function
_source.genAiContent:
# elavon-payment-gateway
## removeClass function
The `removeClass()` function allows you to dynamically remove CSS classes from the secure iframe fields used for payment data entry. This is useful for updating the visual state of fields based on shopper interactions or validation results, helping communicate the status of the field to the shopper.
**When to use removeClass()**
- Remove error styling after the user corrects invalid input.
- Remove temporary classes like `highlighted-field` when the field loses focus.
- Revert fields to their default appearance after certain actions (e.g., switching payment methods).
You can use :magic-link[`getState()`]{id="getstate-function"} to determine the state of the field.
## Syntax
```javascript
const fieldTypes = window.ElavonHostedFields.FieldTypes;
elavonHostedFields.removeClass(fieldTypes.fieldName, 'className');
```
## Parameters
| Parameter
|
Type
| Description |
|----------|-------------|:------|
| fieldTypes | :magic-link[[fieldTypes]]{id="removeclass-function#fieldtypes-object"} | **Required.**
The specific field type to remove the CSS class from. Use one of the properties from FieldTypes (e.g., fieldTypes.cardNumber). |
| className | string | **Required.**
The name of the CSS class to remove. This class should be defined in the styles object when initializing :magic-link[ElavonHostedFields]{id="elavonhostedfields-function#constructor-message-object"} |
### fieldTypes object
The FieldTypes object defines the available hosted fields you can target (e.g., fieldTypes.cardNumber):
| Properties
|
Type
| Description |
|----------|-------------|:------|
| cardNumber | string | Represents the card number input field (PAN). |
| cardholderName | string | Represents the cardholder name input field. |
| cardCvv | string | Represents the CVV (security code) input field. |
| cardExpirationDate | string| Represents the card expiration date input field. |
## Example usage
Remove error class when a field is valid:
:::code-block
```javascript
const fieldTypes = window.ElavonHostedFields.FieldTypes;
const state = elavonHostedFields.getState();
if(state.fields.cardholderName.isValid) {
elavonHostedFields.removeClass(fieldTypes.cardholderName, 'field-error');
}
```
:::

_source.title: setAttribute function
_source.genAiContent:
# elavon-payment-gateway
## setAttribute function
The `setAttribute()` function allows you to dynamically update attributes on the secure iframe fields used for payment data entry. This is useful for customizing the shopper experience by changing labels, placeholders, accessibility attributes, and other UI elements without reloading the page.
**When to use setAttribute()**
- Whenever you need to dynamically update the appearance or behavior of hosted payment fields without reloading the page.
- Update placeholder text (e.g., MM/YY for expiration date) to guide shoppers.
## Syntax
```javascript
const fieldTypes = window.ElavonHostedFields.FieldTypes;
elavonHostedFields.setAttribute(fieldTypes, 'attributeName', 'attributeValue');
```
## Parameters
| Parameter
|
Type
| Description |
|----------|-------------|:------|
| fieldTypes | :magic-link[[fieldTypes]]{id="setattribute-function#fieldtypes-object"} | **Required.**
The specific field type to apply the attribute to. Use one of the properties from FieldTypes (e.g., fieldTypes.cardNumber). |
| attributeName | string | **Required.** See :magic-link[field configuration object]{id="elavonhostedfields-function#field-configuration-object"} for descriptions.
`wrapperId`
`label`
`className`
`ariaLabel`
`placeholder`
`required`
`disabled`
|
| attributeValue | string | **Required.**
The value to assign to the specified attribute. |
### fieldTypes object
The fieldType object represents the iframe fields that you can style with css classes. Each field (e.g., fieldTypes.cardNumber) includes:
| Properties
|
Type
| Description |
|----------|-------------|:------|
| cardNumber | string | Represents the card number input field (PAN). |
| cardholderName | string | Represents the cardholder name input field. |
| cardCvv | string | Represents the CVV (security code) input field. |
| cardExpirationDate | string | Represents the card expiration date input field. |
## Example usage
Here’s a simple example of setting the placeholder text in the expiration date to the value MM/YY:
:::code-block
```javascript
const fieldTypes = window.ElavonHostedFields.FieldTypes;
elavonHostedFields.setAttribute(fieldTypes.cardExpirationDate, 'placeholder', 'MM/YY');
```
:::

_source.title: updateSession function
_source.genAiContent:
# elavon-payment-gateway
## updateSession function
The `updateSession()` function allows you to update the active payment session without re-rendering the hosted fields or digital wallet buttons. This is particularly useful for dynamic checkout experiences where certain session attributes—such as the order total, tax, or shipping costs—may change after the payment form has already been displayed.
By using `updateSession()`, you can keep the payment UI intact while refreshing the underlying session data, ensuring that the payment reflects the latest order details without disrupting the user experience.
**When to use updateSession()**
- Adding/removing items from the cart.
- Updating shipping options or taxes.
- Applying discounts or promotional codes.
## Syntax
```javascript
elavonHostedFields.updateSession(sessionId);
```
### Parameters
| Parameter
|
Type
| Description |
|----------|-------------|:------|
| sessionId | string | **Required.**
The session id from :magic-link[/payment-sessions]{id="/products/elavon-payment-gateway/v1/api-reference#tag/Payment-Sessions/operation/CreatePaymentSession"} |
:::::notice{note}
Some session attributes cannot be modified without creating a new ElavonHostedFields instance. If the new session uses a different *merchant*, *account*, *hppType*, *originUrl*, or attempts to modify the available payment methods, an `invalidPaymentSession` error will be sent via the message handler.
:::::
## Example usage
Here’s an example of updating the hosted fields session without re-rendering:
:::code-block
```javascript
async function updateOrder() {
const response = await fetch('/update-order);
const sessionId = await response.json();
elavonHostedFields.updateSession('sessionId');
}
```
:::

_source.title: destroy function
_source.genAiContent:
# elavon-payment-gateway
## destroy function
The `destroy()` function tears down the Elavon Hosted Fields instance and removes all injected iframes from the DOM. Use it when the payment form is no longer needed—such as navigating away from a checkout step in a Single‑Page App (SPA), closing a modal, or resetting the form before re-initialization.
Once `destroy()` is called:
- All hosted field iframes and related DOM nodes created by the SDK are removed.
- Internal event listeners and message channels are detached.
- The instance is no longer usable. Further method calls (e.g., `show(), addClass(), submit()`) should be considered invalid.
- If you need to show the payment form again (e.g., shopper navigates back), create a new instance.
**When to use destroy()**
- Shopper leaves the payment step and you won’t reuse the same instance.
- Payment modal is dismissed and will be rebuilt next time it opens.
- You plan to re-create hosted fields with new configuration.
## Syntax
```javascript
elavonHostedFields.show();
```
## Example usage
Here’s an example when leaving the payment page or closing a modal:
:::code-block
```javascript
// When leaving the page/closing modal:
function teardownPayment() {
elavonHostedFields.destroy();
}
```
:::

_source.title: show function
_source.genAiContent:
# elavon-payment-gateway
## show function
The `show()` function is used to manually display the payment fields and associated buttons on your page. This gives developers greater control over the user experience, allowing you to decide exactly when the payment form becomes visible.
**When to use show()**
- Use this function only if you have configured `hideUntilRequested: true` in the :magic-link[ElavonHostedFields constructor.]{id="elavonhostedfields-function#message-object"}
- If `hideUntilRequested` is false, the payment fields will automatically appear as soon as the payment session data is fully loaded, and calling `show()` is unnecessary.
- Important: Do not call `show()` until the :magic-link[onReady]{id="onready-function-fields"} callback has fired. This ensures that all hosted fields are initialized and ready to be displayed.
## Syntax
```javascript
elavonHostedFields.show();
```
## Example usage
Here’s a simple example where the payment fields are shown immediately after the integration is ready:
:::code-block
```javascript
function onReady() {
// Hosted fields are initialized and ready
elavonHostedFields.show();
}
```
:::

_source.title: onSurchargeAcknowledgementRequired function
_source.genAiContent:
# elavon-payment-gateway
## onSurchargeAcknowledgementRequired function
The `onSurchargeAcknowledgementRequired()` function allows you to display a prompt to capture consent before proceeding with a surcharge transaction. Include the following information in the acknowledgement using the :magic-link[surchargeInformation]{id="onsurchargeacknowledgementrequired-function#returns"} object:
**Surcharging Notice**
*Please be aware that we will add a surcharge of $0.36 USD to this payment. This surcharge is not greater than our total cost of accepting credit cards. There is no surcharge for debit card payments. By authorizing this payment, you are authorizing us to charge your credit card for a total of $12.36 USD, subject to our terms of service and privacy policy.*
*Do you agree to this charge, including the surcharge?*
**When to use onSurchargeAcknowledgementRequired()**
- If you pass a credit card fee to the cardholder with surcharging.
- To display the surcharging notice.
- To obtain acknowledgment confirming acceptance of the fee.
## Syntax
```javascript
function requestSurchargeAcknowledgement(surchargeInformation, acknowledge) {
//display surcharge message
}
```
## Parameters
| Parameter
|
Type
| Description |
|----------|-------------|:------|
| surchargeInformation | :magic-link[[surchargeInformation]]{id="onsurchargeacknowledgementrequired-function#returns"} | **Required.** Contains the surcharging amount info that must be displayed to the shopper. |
| acknowledge() | function | **Required.** Call this function if the shopper agreed to the surcharge and the transaction should proceed. |
## Returns
### surchargeInformation object
| Properties
|
Type
| Description |
|----------|-------------|:------|
| adjustedTotal | :magic-link[[Total]]{id="onsurchargeacknowledgementrequired-function#total-object"} | Represents the final transaction amount after applying the surcharge. This includes the original total plus any surcharge added. |
| id | object |
A unique identifier for the surcharge information record or transaction. |
| surchargeRate | string
| The percentage rate applied as a surcharge on the transaction.
|
| surchargeTotal |
:magic-link[[Total]]{id="onsurchargeacknowledgementrequired-function#total-object"} | The monetary value of the surcharge applied to the transaction. |
| total |
:magic-link[[Total]]{id="onsurchargeacknowledgementrequired-function#total-object"} | The original transaction amount before any surcharge is applied. |
| guaranteedToBeApplied | boolean
| Indicates whether the surcharge is guaranteed to be applied to the transaction.
|
### Total object
The adjustedTotal, surchargeTotal, total property includes:
| Properties
|
Type
| Description |
|----------|-------------|:------|
| amount | string | The actual value for the object.
|
| currencyCode | string | The ISO 4217 three-letter currency code. |
## Example usage
:::code-block
```javascript
function requestSurchargeAcknowledgement(surchargeInformation, acknowledge) {
// inform user about updated total due to surcharge, call acknowledge function when transaction should proceed
displayUpdatedTotal(surchargeInformation.adjustedTotal).then(() => {
acknowledge();
}
}
```
:::

# elavon-payment-gateway
## Fraud services
Direct API integrators can secure their transactions and reduce their risk of fraud by using Elavon's fraud services API. For more information, refer to the :magic-link[Fraud services documentation]{id="/products/fraud-services/v1/fraud-services-overview"}.

_source.title: Create transaction with surcharge
_source.genAiContent:
# elavon-payment-gateway
## Create Transaction with Surcharge
Surcharge can be added to customer transactions to cover fees charged by card providers. A surcharge advice is used in creating such transactions. It calculates the amount of surcharge and provides an adjusted transaction total for selected card numbers. If a card does not allow surcharge, you can either request a new surcharge advice with a new card, or proceed with that card and not collect surcharge with it.
Surcharge support must be enabled for the merchant and is available for NA merchants only.
## API Integration
For more request and response examples, and for the complete and detailed list of parameters, see the :magic-link[Surcharge Advices]{id="api-reference#tag/Surcharge-Advices"} and :magic-link[Transactions ]{id="api-reference#tag/Transactions"} resources.
### Create surcharge advice
1. Send a Create SurchargeAdvice POST request to the **/surcharge-advices** endpoint.
2. In your request, set the following parameters:
- storedCard - The stored card resource URL. This is obtained from a successful Create StoredCard response.
- hostedCard - The hosted card resource URL. This is obtained from a successful Create HostedCard response.
- hsmCard - The HSM card resource URL. This is obtained from a successful Create HsmCard response.
- cardNumber - The cardholder's personal account number. This value is not returned.
- googlePayPayment - The Google Pay Payment resource URL. This is obtained from a successful Create GooglePayPayment response.
- applePayPayment - The Apple Pay Payment resource URL. This is obtained from a successful Create ApplePayPayment response.
- pazePayment - The Paze Payment resource URL. This is obtained from a successful Create PazePayment response.
- panToken - The personal account number (PAN) token. It contains the fields below:
- token - The tokenized representation of a sensitive value, like a credit card number. This is required.
- provider - The provider or method that generated the token. This is required.
- type - The type of token as defined by the provider. This is required.
- total - The total amount of the transaction. This is required. It contains the fields below:
- amount - The actual value of the transaction. This is required.
- currencyCode - The ISO 4217 three-letter currency code. This is required.
- shopperInteraction - The type of interaction with the customer that generated this transaction. Valid values are the following: ecommerce (default), mailOrder, telephoneOrder, merchantInitiated, inPerson. This is required.
3. Exactly one of the following is required to create a surcharge advice request: storedCard, hostedCard, hsmCard, cardNumber, googlePayPayment, applePayPayment, pazePayment, or panToken.
4. Wait for the response from the /surcharge-advices endpoint. In the response, take note of the following:
- id - The surcharge advice resource ID. This is used for retrieving the surcharge advice.
- href - The surcharge advice resource URL. This will be used for creating the actual transaction.
:::::notice{note}
Any surcharge must be performed first before creating a forex advice. The adjustedTotal value from a successful Create SurchargeAdvice response becomes the total value for a Create ForexAdvice request.
:::::
#### Example
The following is an example of a JSON request for a surcharge advice in the amount of USD 5.00.
```json
POST "https://uat.api.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/surcharge-advices"
{
"total":
{
"amount":
"5.00",
"currencyCode":
"USD"
},
"cardNumber": "XXXXXXXXXXXX4444"
}
```
The following is an example of a successful JSON response to the request above.
```json
{
"href":
"https://uat.api.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/surcharge-advices/3kbjcwpb6yyj7qkbq77ttfdhfytv",
"id":
"3kbjcwpb6yyj7qkbq77ttfdhfytv",
"createdAt":
"2024-02-05T16:53:24.449Z",
"expiresAt":
"2024-02-05T17:23:24.449Z",
"merchant":
"https://uat.api.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/merchants/28kvwjbm67k36kxcvw6rgwdggkf6",
"processorAccount":
"https://uat.api.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/processor-accounts/prrgxhfwxw4ptwxvqdp7q9qydbd9",
"hsmCard":
null,
"panToken":
{
"token":
"9467275901658927495",
"provider":
"ELAVON_C2_V1",
"type":
"pan"
},
"maskedNumber":
"XXXXXXXXXXXX4444",
"last4":
"4444",
"bin":
"425702",
"panFingerprint":
"f8gcmwwc7g998tgqqm8pp23pdt9f",
"total":
{
"amount":
"5.00",
"currencyCode":
"USD"
},
"surchargeRate":
"0.03",
"surchargeTotal":
{
"amount":
"0.15",
"currencyCode":
"USD"
},
"adjustedTotal":
{
"amount":
"5.15",
"currencyCode":
"USD"
},
"customReference":
null,
"customFields":
null
}
```
If the storedCard, hostedCard, cardNumber, or panToken is not eligible for surcharge, the following error response will be received:
```json
{
"status" : 400,
"failures" : [ {
"code" : "badRequest",
"description" : "The request is invalid; correct all issues before resending",
"field" : null
}, {
"code" : "surchargeNotSupported",
"description" : "Surcharging is not supported for this card",
"field" : null
} ]
}
```
### Create transaction with surcharge advice
1. Send a Create Transaction POST request to the **/transactions** endpoint.
2. In your request, set the following parameters:
- type - The transaction type. The default value is "sale".
- surchargeAdvice - The surcharge advice resource URL. This is obtained from a successful Create SurchargeAdvice response. This is not returned.
- orderReference - The order reference number for the transaction.
- shopperInteraction - The type of interaction with the customer that generated this transaction. Valid values are the following: ecommerce (default), mailOrder, telephoneOrder, merchantInitiated, inPerson. Note that using the default option will not authorize if the 3DS details are not provided.
- card - The customer's card details. It contains the fields below:
- holderName - The customer's full name.
- number - The cardholder's personal account number. This value is not returned.
- expirationMonth - The card's expiration month. This is required.
- expirationYear - The card's expiration year. This is required.
- securityCode - The card's security code. This is required and the value is not returned.
- billTo - The customer's billing address. It contains the fields below:
- street1 - The first address line.
- street2 - The second address line.
- city - The town or city.
- postalCode - The zip or postal code. This is required for UK merchants.
- countryCode - The ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3 country code.
- doCapture - The action to be used on the transaction. If set to true (default), the transaction will capture for settlement. If set to false, the transaction will authorize only.
- doSendReceipt - If set to true, receipt will be sent to the customer's email. If set to false (default), receipt will not be emailed.
3. Wait for the response from the /transactions endpoint. In the response, take note of the following:
- id - The transaction resource ID. This is used for retrieving and updating the transaction.
- href - The transaction resource URL.
- surcharge - The surcharge details, which include the unadjusted total, surcharge total, and surcharge rate.
- isAuthorized - Indicates whether the transaction is authorized. Sale transactions are authorized by the gateway, processor, and issuer.
:::::notice{note}
The total amount for the transaction is no longer specified because this value will be derived from the surcharge advice.
:::::
#### Example
The following is an example of a JSON request for a USD 5.00 transaction with a surcharge advice created earlier.
```json
POST "https://uat.api.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/transactions"
{
"surchargeAdvice":
"https://uat.api.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/surcharge-advices/3kbjcwpb6yyj7qkbq77ttfdhfytv",
"shopperInteraction": "telephoneOrder",
"card":
{
"holderName":
"John Doe",
"number":
"XXXXXXXXXXXX4444",
"expirationMonth":
3,
"expirationYear":
2027
}
}
```
The following is an example of a successful JSON response to the request above.

## Back Office UI
### Create a Transaction with Surcharge
When creating a transaction, if the selected card allows surcharge, this notice is displayed at the Card Details section of the Create Transaction page. If you process this transaction, the surcharge will be calculated, including the adjusted total of the transaction. You will then be prompted to read the surcharge disclosure to the cardholder. Once acknowledged, the surcharge calculation will be passed to the transaction.
For more information on creating transactions, see the :magic-link[Sale Transaction]{id="sale_transaction"} and :magic-link[Stored Shoppers and Stored Cards]{id="stored-shoppers-and-cards"} pages.

_source.title: Refund transaction with surcharge
_source.genAiContent:
# elavon-payment-gateway
## Refund Transaction with Surcharge
Refunding a transaction with surcharge returns the money to the customer, including the surcharge. You can perform either a partial or full refund on a transaction with surcharge.
Surcharge support must be enabled for the merchant and is available for NA merchants only.
## API Integration
For more request and response examples, and for the complete and detailed list of parameters, see the :magic-link[Refund Surcharge Advices]{id="api-reference#tag/Refund-Surcharge-Advices"} and :magic-link[Transactions ]{id="api-reference#tag/Transactions"} resources.
### Create refund surcharge advice
1. Send a Create RefundSurchargeAdvice POST request to the **/refund-surcharge-advices** endpoint.
2. In your request, set the following parameters:
- parentTransaction - The transaction resource URL. This is required.
- total - The total amount of the transaction. This is only required when processing a partial refund. It contains the fields below.
- amount - The actual value of the transaction. This is required.
- currencyCode - The ISO 4217 three-letter currency code. This is required.
3. Wait for the response from the /refund-surcharge-advices endpoint. In the response, take note of the following:
- id - The refund surcharge advice resource ID. This is used for retrieving the refund surcharge advice.
- href - The refund surcharge advice resource URL. This will be used for creating the actual refund.
:::::notice{note}
Refund surcharge advice is only needed for partial refunds of transactions with surcharge. When doing a full refund, perform a regular refund transaction instead. For more information, see :magic-link[Refund Transaction]{id="refund_transaction"}.
:::::
#### Example
The following is an example of a JSON request for a refund surcharge advice in the amount of USD 2.00.
```json
POST "https://uat.api.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/refund-surcharge-advices"
{
"parentTransaction": "https://uat.api.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/transactions/mth4yjfcqh99bckmmhvrx8rh8hv4",
"total":
{
"amount":
"2.00",
"currencyCode":
"USD"
}
}
```
The following is an example of a successful JSON response to the request above.
```json
{
"href":
"https://uat.api.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/refund-surcharge-advices/qx998t74dc4qd4wv72t74p2dw4cq",
"id":
"qx998t74dc4qd4wv72t74p2dw4cq",
"createdAt":
"2024-02-05T16:58:00.522Z",
"expiresAt":
"2024-02-05T17:28:00.522Z",
"merchant":
"https://uat.api.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/merchants/28kvwjbm67k36kxcvw6rgwdggkf6",
"processorAccount":
"https://uat.api.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/processor-accounts/prrgxhfwxw4ptwxvqdp7q9qydbd9",
"parentTransaction":
"https://uat.api.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/transactions/mth4yjfcqh99bckmmhvrx8rh8hv4",

{

"2.00",

"USD"
},

"USD"
},

{

"2.06",
"currencyCode":
"USD"
},

null,
"customFields":
null

```
### Create transaction with refund surcharge advice
1. Send a Create Transaction POST request to the **/transactions** endpoint.
2. In your request, set the following parameters:
- type - The transaction type. This is required. Set this field to "refund".
- refundSurchargeAdvice - The refund surcharge advice resource URL. This is obtained from a successful Create RefundSurchargeAdvice response. This is not returned.
- parentTransaction - The transaction resource URL. This is required.
- shopperInteraction - The type of interaction with the customer that generated this transaction. Valid values are the following: ecommerce (default), mailOrder, telephoneOrder, merchantInitiated.
3. Wait for the response from the /transactions endpoint. In the response, take note of the following:
- id - The transaction resource ID. This is used for retrieving and updating the transaction.
- href - The transaction resource URL.
- surcharge - The surcharge details, which include the unadjusted total, surcharge total, and surcharge rate.
- isAuthorized - Indicates whether the transaction is authorized. Refunds are authorized by the gateway.
:::::notice{note}
The total amount for the transaction is no longer specified because this value will be derived from the refund surcharge advice.
:::::
#### Example
The following is an example of a JSON request for a partial refund as calculated by the refund surcharge advice created earlier.
```json
POST "https://uat.api.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/transactions"
{
"type": "refund",
"refundSurchargeAdvice":
"https://uat.api.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/refund-surcharge-advices/qx998t74dc4qd4wv72t74p2dw4cq",
"parentTransaction": "https://uat.api.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/transactions/mth4yjfcqh99bckmmhvrx8rh8hv4",
"shopperInteraction": "telephoneOrder"
}
```
The following is an example of a successful JSON response to the request above.

## Back Office UI
### Refund a transaction with surcharge
At the Back Office UI, refunding a transaction can only be performed by users with a Manager or Admin role. Only transactions that have already been settled can be refunded.
When refunding a transaction with surcharge, enter the amount to be refunded to the customer without including the surcharge. The surcharge to be refunded will be automatically calculated once the refund is processed.
For more information on refunding transactions, see :magic-link[Refund Transaction]{id="refund_transaction"}.

_source.title: Dynamic currency conversion
_source.genAiContent:
# elavon-payment-gateway
## Dynamic Currency Conversion
A forex advice tracks the exchange rate between the currency of the card used in a transaction and the settlement currency of the merchant. The settlement currency is set during the merchant's onboarding process and it cannot be changed. Note that forex advices are only available for merchant accounts with dynamic currency conversion (DCC) enabled.
## API Integration
For more request and response examples, and for the complete and detailed list of parameters, see the :magic-link[Forex Advices]{id="api-reference#tag/Forex-Advices"} and :magic-link[Transactions]{id="api-reference#tag/Transactions"} resources.
### Create forex advice
1. Send a Create ForexAdvice POST request to the **/forex-advices** endpoint.
2. In your request, set the following parameters:
- storedCard - The stored card resource URL. This is obtained from a successful Create StoredCard response.
- hostedCard - The hosted card resource URL. This is obtained from a successful Create HostedCard response.
- cardNumber - The cardholder's personal account number. This value is not returned.
- panToken - The personal account number (PAN) token. It contains the fields below:
- token - The tokenized representation of a sensitive value, like a credit card number. This is required.
- provider - The provider or method that generated the token. This is required.
- type - The type of token as defined by the provider. This is required.
- total - The total amount of the transaction in the merchant’s settlement currency. This is required. It contains the fields below:
- amount - The actual value of the transaction. This is required.
- currencyCode - The ISO 4217 three-letter currency code. This is required.
- shopperInteraction - The type of interaction with the customer that generated this transaction. Valid values are the following: ecommerce (default), mailOrder, telephoneOrder, merchantInitiated. This is required.
3. Exactly one of the following is required to create a forex advice request: storedCard, hostedCard, cardNumber, or panToken.
4. Wait for the response from the /forex-advices endpoint. In the response, take note of the following:
- id - The forex advice resource ID. This is used for retrieving the forex advice.
- href - The forex advice resource URL. This will be used for creating the actual transaction.
:::::notice{note}
Any surcharge must be performed first before creating a forex advice. The adjustedTotal value from a successful Create SurchargeAdvice response becomes the total value for a Create ForexAdvice request.
:::::
#### Example
The following is an example of a JSON request for a forex advice in the amount of USD 3.00.
```json
POST "https://uat.api.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/forex-advices"
{
"total":
{
"amount":
"3.00",
"currencyCode":
"USD"
},
"cardNumber": "XXXXXXXXXXXX0000",
"shopperInteraction": "telephoneOrder"
}
```
The following is an example of a successful JSON response to the request above.
```json
{
"href":
"https://uat.api.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/forex-advices/p2hck3h4g9bcdm2hr9yvycppjm8t",
"id":
"p2hck3h4g9bcdm2hr9yvycppjm8t",
"createdAt":
"2024-01-29T18:59:07.900Z",
"expiresAt":
"2024-01-30T18:59:07.881Z",
"merchant":
"https://uat.api.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/merchants/28kvwjbm67k36kxcvw6rgwdggkf6",
"panToken":
{
"token":
"9580733899373269555",
"provider":
"ELAVON_C2_V1",
"type":
"pan"
},
"maskedNumber":
"XXXXXXXXXXXX0000",
"last4":
"0000",
"bin":
"512079",
"panFingerprint":
"m7bh97bm36tpt6bdhgphfv8vdwdt",
"total":
{
"amount":
"3.00",
"currencyCode":
"USD"
},
"issuerTotal":
{
"amount":
"405",
"currencyCode":
"JPY"
},
"conversionRate":
"135.05698",
"markupRate":
"0.0325",
"markupRateAnnotation":
"none",
"shopperInteraction":
"telephoneOrder",
"customReference":
null,
"customFields":
null
}
```
If the storedCard, hostedCard, cardNumber, or panToken is not eligible for DCC, the following error response will be received:
```json
{
"status" : 400,
"failures" : [ {
"code" : "badRequest",
"description" : "The request is invalid; correct all issues before resending",
"field" : null
}, {
"code" : "currencyConversionUnavailable",
"description" : "Currency conversion is unavailable for this card",
"field" : null
} ]
}
```
### Create transaction with forex advice
1. Send a Create Transaction POST request to the **/transactions** endpoint.
2. In your request, set the following parameters:
- type - The transaction type. The default value is "sale".
- forexAdvice - The forex advice resource URL. This is obtained from a successful Create ForexAdvice response. This is not returned.
- orderReference - The order reference number for the transaction.
- shopperInteraction - The type of interaction with the customer that generated this transaction. Valid values are the following: ecommerce (default), mailOrder, telephoneOrder, merchantInitiated. Note that using the default option will not authorize if the 3DS details are not provided.
- card - The customer's card details. It contains the fields below:
- holderName - The customer's full name.
- number - The cardholder's personal account number. This value is not returned.
- expirationMonth - The card's expiration month. This is required.
- expirationYear - The card's expiration year. This is required.
- securityCode - The card's security code. This is required and the value is not returned.
- billTo - The customer's billing address. It contains the fields below:
- street1 - The first address line.
- street2 - The second address line.
- city - The town or city.
- postalCode - The zip or postal code. This is required for UK merchants.
- countryCode - The ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3 country code.
- doCapture - The action to be used on the transaction. If set to true (default), the transaction will capture for settlement. If set to false, the transaction will authorize only.
- doSendReceipt - If set to true, receipt will be sent to the customer's email. If set to false (default), receipt will not be emailed.
3. Wait for the response from the /transactions endpoint. In the response, take note of the following:
- id - The transaction resource ID. This is used for retrieving and updating the transaction.
- href - The transaction resource URL.
- isAuthorized - Indicates whether the transaction is authorized. Sale transactions are authorized by the gateway, processor, and issuer.
:::::notice{note}
The total amount for the transaction is no longer specified because this value will be derived from the forex advice.
:::::
#### Example
The following is an example of a JSON request for a USD 3.00 transaction with a forex advice created earlier.
```json
POST "https://uat.api.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/transactions"
{
"forexAdvice":
"https://uat.api.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/forex-advices/p2hck3h4g9bcdm2hr9yvycppjm8t",
"shopperInteraction": "telephoneOrder",
"card":
{
"holderName":
"John Doe",
"number":
"XXXXXXXXXXXX0000",
"expirationMonth":
3,
"expirationYear":
2027
}
}
```
The following is an example of a successful JSON response to the request above.
```json
{
"href":
"https://uat.api.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/transactions/t6hrfb268tvcyqkhpcqmxfy87gm6",
```
