## Back Office UI
### Create a Transaction with Dynamic Currency Conversion
When creating a transaction, if the selected card is eligible for dynamic currency conversion (DCC), you can select which currency to use in processing the transaction. If the selected currency is the home currency of the card, the system will perform a currency conversion and you will be prompted to confirm the DCC acceptance with the cardholder. The cardholder can accept or decline the currency conversion. If accepted, the cardholder is charged a markup on the transaction total that should be disclosed to them before accepting. Once the DCC acceptance is confirmed, the currency conversion performed will be passed to the transaction.
For more information on creating transactions, see :magic-link[Sale Transaction]{id="sale_transaction"} and :magic-link[Stored Shopper and Stored Cards]{id="stored-shoppers-and-cards"}.

_source.title: Hosted cards
_source.genAiContent:
# elavon-payment-gateway
## Hosted Cards
A hosted card is a single-use card token that is returned by a successful checkout using a payment session. When using an end-to-end transaction, the hosted payments page (HPP) uses the hosted card to immediately create a transaction for the concluded payment session.
You have the option to disable end-to-end transactions and have the HPP post a hosted card's resource URI to an endpoint on your server.
## Hosted Card Transactions
A hosted card transaction can be started by setting doCreateTransaction to *false* when you create a payment session. Instead of creating a transaction for the payment session after capturing payment information from the shopper, EPG returns a hosted card resource URI to an endpoint you provide.
:::::notice{note}
Hosted cards are only valid for 30 minutes. If you use a hosted card after 30 minutes from its creation, the transaction will fail.
:::::
For more information, see the :magic-link[Hosted Cards]{id="api-reference#tag/Hosted-Cards"} and the :magic-link[Payment Sessions]{id="api-reference#tag/Payment-Sessions"} resources.
### 3D Secure
A reason to consider disabling end to end transactions is if your business case needs different security checks than the default 3D Secure check EPG provides. The hosted card masks sensitive fields of information but provides the required fields to perform custom 3-D Secure validations.
### Lightbox
When using the lightbox, you will need to set the form you use to initiate a lightbox checkout to post the information returned from a successful checkout to an endpoint that is prepared to handle the process.
```xml
```
### Redirect
With a redirect, the information is returned as a query parameter sent to the URL you provide as the returnUrl property of the payment session.
```json
{
"order": "https://api.na.elavonpayments.com/orders/p7hxrvrpgfc68w6gm7q3b47y",
"returnUrl": "https://merchant.com/return",
"cancelUrl": "https://merchant.com/cancel"
}
```
A payment session created with a body holding the previous example would return the shopper to your website by placing a get request to the following URL: `https://merchant.com/return?token=`
### Create the transaction
To create a transaction, send a Create Transaction POST request to the **/transactions** endpoint. The body of the post only requires the payment session's resource URL. This is obtained from a successful Create PaymentSession response.
Below is an example on how to create a transaction from the UAT environment:
```json
POST "https://uat.api.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/transactions"
{
"paymentSession": "https://uat.api.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/payment-sessions/rd8y9xhx7qh9yj6r4vpxpqcv"
}
```
For forward compatibility, do not include hostedCard in the request. Only include paymentSession and any other information.
For more information, see the :magic-link[Transactions]{id="api-reference#tag/Transactions"} and the :magic-link[Payment Sessions]{id="api-reference#tag/Payment-Sessions"} resources.

_source.title: Stored shoppers and stored cards
_source.genAiContent:
# elavon-payment-gateway
## Stored Shoppers and Stored Cards
Save a customer and their card details. Once these are saved, they can be referenced for easier processing of transactions. In the Back Office UI, API shoppers are referred to as customers but they are the same resource.
## API Integration
For more request and response examples, and for the complete and detailed list of parameters, see the :magic-link[Shoppers]{id="api-reference#tag/Shoppers"}, :magic-link[Stored Cards]{id="api-reference#tag/Stored-Cards"}, and :magic-link[Transactions]{id="api-reference#tag/Transactions"} resources.
### Create shopper
1. Send a Create Shopper POST request to the **/shoppers** endpoint.
2. In your request, set the following parameters:
- fullName - The name of the customer. This is required.
- description - Any additional information about the customer.
- company - The customer's company name.
- primaryAddress - The customer's address that can be used for shipping and billing. It contains the fields below:
- street1 - The first address line.
- street2 - The second address line.
- city - The town or city.
- postalCode - The zip or postal code.
- countryCode - The ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3 country code.
- primaryPhone - The customer's phone number.
- email - The customer's email address.
3. Wait for the response from the /shoppers endpoint. In the response, take note of the following:
- id - The shopper resource ID. This is used for retrieving and updating the shopper.
- href - The shopper resource URL. This is used for associating the shopper with a stored card.
#### Example
The following is an example of a JSON request for creating a shopper named John Doe.
```json
POST "https://uat.api.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/shoppers"
{
"fullName": "John Doe",
"primaryAddress":
{
"street1":
"123 Baker St.",
"street2":
"Suite B",
"city":
"London",
"region":
"England",
"postalCode":
"NW1 6XE",
"countryCode":
"GBR"
},
"primaryPhone": "+44 020 7946 0123",
"email": "john.doe@email.com"
}
```
The following is an example of a successful JSON response to the request above.
```json
{
"href":
"https://uat.api.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/shoppers/pbrcccxy9pgk4qfdcbhf88w97hxv",

```
### Create stored card
1. Send a Create StoredCard POST request to the **/stored-cards** endpoint.
2. In your request, set the following parameters:
- shopper - The shopper resource URL. This is obtained from a successful Create Shopper response. This is required.
- card - The stored card details. It contains the fields below:
- holderName - The stored cardholder's full name.
- number - The stored card's personal account number. This value is not returned.
- expirationMonth - The stored card's expiration month. This is required.
- expirationYear - The stored card's expiration year. This is required.
- securityCode - The stored card's security code. This is required and the value is not returned.
- billTo - The stored card's billing address. It contains the fields below:
- street1 - The first address line.
- street2 - The second address line.
- city - The town or city.
- postalCode - The zip or postal code. This is required for UK merchants.
- countryCode - The ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3 country code.
3. Wait for the response from the /stored-cards endpoint. In the response, take note of the following:
- id - The stored card resource ID. This is used for retrieving and updating the stored card.
- href - The stored card resource URL. This is used for associating the stored card with a shopper.
#### Example
The following is an example of a JSON request for creating a stored card under John Doe.
```json
POST "https://uat.api.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/stored-cards"
{
"shopper": "https://uat.api.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/shoppers/pbrcccxy9pgk4qfdcbhf88w97hxv",
"card":
{
"holderName":
"John Doe",
"number": "5100.0800.0004.1251",
"expirationMonth":
1,
"expirationYear":
2025,
"securityCode": "123",
"billTo":
{
"street1":
"123 Baker St.",
"street2":
"Suite B",
"city":
"London",
"region":
"England",
"postalCode":
"NW1 6XE",
"countryCode":
"GBR"
}
}
}
```
The following is an example of a successful JSON response to the request above.

### Create transaction with a stored card
1. Send a Create Transaction POST request to the **/transactions** endpoint.
2. In your request, set the following parameters:
- type - The transaction type. The default value is "sale".
- total - The total amount of the transaction. It contains the fields below:
- amount - The actual value of the transaction. This is required.
- currencyCode - The ISO 4217 three-letter currency code. This is required.
- orderReference - The order reference number for the transaction.
- shopperInteraction - The type of interaction with the customer that generated this transaction. Valid values are the following: ecommerce (default), mailOrder, telephoneOrder, merchantInitiated. Note that using the default option will not authorize if the 3DS details are not provided.
- shopper - The shopper resource URL. This is obtained from a successful Create Shopper response.
- storedCard - The stored card resource URL. This is obtained from a successful Create StoredCard response.
- doCapture - The action to be used on the transaction. If set to true (default), the transaction will capture for settlement. If set to false, the transaction will authorize only.
- doSendReceipt - If set to true, receipt will be sent to the customer's email. If set to false (default), receipt will not be emailed.
3. Wait for the response from the /transactions endpoint. In the response, take note of the following:
- id - The transaction resource ID. This is used for retrieving and updating the transaction.
- href - The transaction resource URL.
- isAuthorized - Indicates whether the transaction is authorized. Sale transactions are authorized by the gateway, processor, and issuer.
#### Example
The following is an example of a JSON request for a USD 20.00 payment using the stored card created under John Doe.
```json
POST "https://uat.api.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/transactions"

{

"20.00",

"USD"
},

"storedCard": "https://uat.api.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/stored-cards/g9fw9kg2fjfcdm62rb2f38cqmx9m"
}
```

## Back Office UI
### Create a customer
1. Click the **Customers** menu item.
2. Click the **New Customer** button. The **New Customer Profile** window appears.
3. Enter the **Customer Name** and **Email Address**.
4. Enter the **Company** name, **Phone Number**, and any **Additional Info**, if required.
5. If you are entering the Phone Number, select the appropriate **Country Code** from the dropdown list.
6. To save the customer's shipping address, do the following:
1. Toggle the **Shipping Address** button. Additional fields appear.
2. Select the **Country** from the dropdown list.
3. Enter the **Address Line**, **Town/City**, and **Postal Code** in the corresponding fields.
7. Click the **Create Profile** button to save the customer details.
### Add card to a customer
1. Click the **Customers** menu item.
2. Select the customer whose card details you want to add. The corresponding customer profile appears.
3. In the **Wallet** section of the customer profile, click the **Add Card** button. The **Add Payment Card** window appears.
4. Review and edit the **Cardholder Name**, if required.
5. Enter the **Card Number** and the **Expiry Date** in MM/YY format.
6. Enter the card's **Security Code**.
7. To save the customer's billing address, do the following:
a. Toggle the **Billing Address** button. Additional fields appear.
b. Select the **Country** from the dropdown list.
c. Enter the **Address Line**, **Town/City**, and **Postal Code** in the corresponding fields.
8. Click the **Add to Profile** button to add the card details to the customer profile.
### Create a transaction
1. See steps 1 to 7 under :magic-link[Create a Transaction in the Sale Transaction]{id="sale_transaction#create-a-transaction"} page.
2. In the **Customer** section, search for the customer profile by doing one of the following:
- In the **Search for a customer** field, enter a part of the customer's name and select the profile from the results.
- Select the customer profile from the **Search for a customer** dropdown list.
3. In the **Payment** section, select the card to be used from the **Payment Method** dropdown list.
4. Review the customer profile and card details that are populated in the fields and edit, if required.
5. Click the **Process Transaction** button to proceed with the transaction. The transaction details will appear after the transaction is processed.
### Create a transaction from a customer's profile
1. Click the **Customers** menu item.
2. Select the customer you will create a transaction for. The corresponding customer profile appears.
3. Click the **Actions** button, then select **New Transaction**. The **New Transaction** form appears with the customer's details automatically populated.
4. See steps 4 to 6 under :magic-link[Create a Transaction]{id="sale_transaction#create-a-transaction"}.
5. Click the **Process Transaction** button to proceed with the transaction. The transaction details will appear after the transaction is processed.
:::::notice{note}
Depending on your account settings, you may need to select a currency for the transaction amount. If your account is set to use only one currency, the default currency will be used.
:::::

_source.title: Payment method capture
_source.genAiContent:
# elavon-payment-gateway
## Payment Method Capture
Elavon Payment Gateway allows capturing a shopper's payment information/card details even without a transaction (i.e. no order required) with the **Payment Method Link** and **Payment Method Session** resources.
## Payment Method Session
The Payment Method Session resource allows an HPP to create a new or select an existing :magic-link[Stored Card]{id="api-reference#tag/Stored-Cards"} resource without the need for a transaction.
For more request and
response examples, and for the complete and detailed list of parameters, see the :magic-link[Payment Method Session]{id="api-reference#tag/Payment-Method-Sessions"} resource.
### Create Payment Method Session
1.
Send a POST request to the **/payment-method-sessions** endpoint.
2. In the request, the following parameters can be set:
- expiresAt - expiration of the payment method session
- hppType - the type of HPP for the payment session, default is *fullPageRedirect*. Other values include paymentLink, lightbox, and hostedPaymentFields.
- returnUrl - URL to redirect to after payment details are collected.
- cancelUrl - URL to redirect to if shopper cancels.
- originUrl - URL where the HPP will be embedded. Required if hppType is *lightbox*.
- shopper - Unique identifier of the shopper's information.
The returnUrl and cancelUrl parameters are required if hppType is *fullPageRedirect*, and must be null if hppType is *lightbox* or *hostedPaymentFields*.
3.
Wait for the response from the /payment-method-sessions endpoint.
## Payment Method Link
The Payment Method Link resource creates a URL that takes the shopper to a payment method session HPP.
For more request and
response examples, and for the complete and detailed list of parameters, see the :magic-link[Payment Method Link]{id="api-reference#tag/Payment-Method-Links"} resource.
### Create Payment Method Link
1.
Send a POST request to the **/payment-method-links** endpoint.
2. In the request, set the following parameters.
- returnUrl -
URL to redirect to after payment details are collected.
- expiresAt - expiration of the payment method link
- description - description of the payment method link.
- shopper - Unique identifier of the shopper's information.
3.
Wait for the response from the /payment-method-links endpoint.
### Update Payment Method Link
1. Send an Update Payment Method Link POST request to **/payment-method-links/{id}**, where **id** is the payment method link's resource id. Below is an example of a complete URL where to send the request:
`https://uat.api.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/payment-method-links/kuTrabraclvAclqAh3seqitheMeb`
2. In your request, set the following parameter:
- doCancel - This determines the status of the payment method link. If set to true, the payment method link is cancelled. If set to false (default), the payment method link remains active.
3. Wait for the response from the **/payment-method-links** endpoint. In the response, take note of the following:
- cancelledAt - The date and time when the payment link was cancelled.

_source.title: Webhook notifications
_source.genAiContent:
# elavon-payment-gateway
## Webhook Notifications
Elavon Payment Gateway uses pull and push notifications to inform you about relevant events happening within our platform. For example, each time you authorize, capture, or settle a transaction, we generate a notification.
EPG supports notifications for the following resources: transactions, payment links, and payment sessions. Below are the supported event types for each resource.
### Supported event types
| Resource | Event Types|
|---|---|
| Transaction | saleDeclined
saleAuthorized
saleHeldForReview
saleCaptured
saleSettled
voidDeclined
voidAuthorized
refundDeclined
refundAuthorized
refundCaptured
refundSettled |
| Payment session | expired
reset
saleDeclined
saleAuthorized
saleAuthorizationPending |
| Payment link | expired
saleDeclined
saleAuthorized
saleAuthorizationPending |
## Pull Notifications
A pull notification allows you to capture transaction details by calling our external API with your merchant alias and merchant secret key. You can use the GET method to pull notifications. The API provides a complete list of notifications that match your merchant identifier and not just the newest ones. It is possible to a filter on the "*createdAt*" field to ensure that only notifications before or after a certain date are pulled.
Check out the following GET method examples for more information.
- `GET https://api.na.elavonpayments.com/notifications` - pulls all notifications.
- `GEThttps://api.na.elavonpayments.com/notifications?filter=createdAt_ge_2023-11-01T09:15:01.260Z` - pulls all notifications after 2023-11-01T09:15:01.260Z. The "ge" implies "greater than or equals to". The other options are "gt", "le", "lt", and "eq" which signify "greater than only", “less than or equals to", "less than only", and "equals" respectively.
- `GET https://api.na.elavonpayments.com/notifications?filter=createdAt_ge_2023-11-01T09:15:01.260Z&filter=createdAt_le_2023-11-01T15:50:04.110Z` - pulls all notifications after 2023-11-01T09:15:01.260Z and before 2023-11-01T15:50:04.110Z. This example shows how you can combine two or more filters into one GET request.
You do not need to contact support to request a pull notification.
## Push Notifications
You can set up a webhook that allows EPG to push notifications about transaction events to a secure URL. If the push is not successful for any reason, we will automatically retry according to the following schedule:
- 30 seconds
- 5 minutes
- 1 hour
- 24 hours
After retrying four times on that schedule, the webhook disables itself until you contact support to re-enable it. Once support re-enables the webhook, EPG will attempt to push transaction event notifications again to that webhook's endpoint. In this case, only notifications from the last two weeks are retained and anything beyond that is lost.
To confirm that you have processed the notification properly, please return a 201 HTTP status message to avoid retries.
## Security Features for Push Notifications
### Webhook
Reach out to support with an HTTPS URL that you would like to receive notifications on and support will whitelist this URL for you on the backend. Once this URL is ready to be set up to listen for notifications, EPG will send notifications in the form of a POST.
Here is a sample curl of what to expect for integrator-side testing:
```json
curl --request POST \
--url "" \
--header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
--header 'Authorization: Basic bXlFbGF2b25XZWJob29rU2VydmljZTplM01uTiE/cGY0' \
--header 'signature-2v24rpkw4hjm8p73vfqx7cwrx7w7: xv2w3BHPQkJpZzEedvGONUoN7ZgQnZJVuKVYtTUeVvrE4GfWX79SQpohNVVepbr55iO7Em6R1rRsQDRSzYgA9w== ' \
--data '{
"href": "https://api.eu.elavonpayments.com/notifications/j8t32fd7p29twgyj2fcb6dd4fdpw",
"id": "j8t32fd7p29twgyj2fcb6dd4fdpw",
"merchant": "https://api.eu.elavonpayments.com/merchants/3x7gwcy33vb84hb732pr44fvfkqh",
"createdAt": "2023-10-31T20:59:45.210Z",
"eventType": "saleAuthorized",
"resourceType": "transaction",
"resource": "https://api.eu.elavonpayments.com/transactions/ch7g7gmmxfgyvq7thkrm3ccdk3gy"
}'
```
Ensure that the webhook server that hosts the endpoint we will send the POST to is set up to accept application/json as its *Content-Type*. The *Authorization* and *signature-* headers are optional, and depend on the setup and subsequent configuration of the webhook resource.
The signer ID in the example is *2v24rpkw4hjm8p73vfqx7cwrx7w7* and the notification signature is *xv2w3BHPQkJpZzEedvGONUoN7ZgQnZJVuKVYtTUeVvrE4GfWX79SQpohNVVepbr55iO7Em6R1rRsQDRSzYgA9w*. Note that these are dummy values and should not be used as test cases or the like. There are more concrete examples below.
### Webhook security
There are multiple levels of security you can have on your webhook endpoint to ensure the notification belongs to you:
-
No authentication. This is not recommended.
-
Authorization header containing the encrypted username and password combination.
-
Signer header containing a signed value that must match a signed value provided by support to store on your end.
-
Using the second and third options together.
#### Webhook security variations
1. A webhook without authentication
Pass your desired webhook URL for EPG to set up for you. Once that is ready, you'll receive a notification on that endpoint from us. This will contain no additional security headers and therefore no additional measures, although this is the easiest to implement. It is recommended to call the GET endpoints specified in the pull notifications section above to ensure that you are receiving authentic notifications to avoid any potential spoofs. Ensure that the ID for notifications there match what you see in your received notifications. Not following this verification is not advisable.
2. A webhook with authentication
If you'd like a username and password to be associated with your webhook, please pass that along with the URL during setup. These credentials will be in the authorization header of the pushed notification, and it is up to the you to decrypt these credentials to ensure that the notification belongs to them. You may pass a username with a blank password as well if desired.
The details of this HTTP basic authentication can be found here: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc7617.
The code below delineates how to decrypt this basic authorization header:
```java
if (authorizationHeader != null)
{
String credentialsUsername = webhook.getCredentialsUsername();
String credentialsPassword = webhook.getCredentialsPassword();
if (authorizationHeader.startsWith("Basic "))
{
try
{
authorizationHeader = authorizationHeader.replace("Basic ", "");
byte[] decodedBytes = Base64.getDecoder().decode(authorizationHeader);
String decodedBasicAuthorization = new String(decodedBytes, StandardCharsets.UTF_8);
String[] basicAuthorizationSplit = decodedBasicAuthorization.split(":");
if (basicAuthorizationSplit.length != 2 ||
!credentialsUsername.equals(basicAuthorizationSplit[0]) ||
!credentialsPassword.equals(basicAuthorizationSplit[1]))
{
responseHttpStatus = HttpStatus.UNAUTHORIZED;
}
}
catch (Exception e)
{
log.error("Exception during basic authorization for webhook notification", e);
responseHttpStatus = HttpStatus.UNAUTHORIZED;
}
}
else if (credentialsUsername != null || credentialsPassword != null)
{
responseHttpStatus = HttpStatus.UNAUTHORIZED;
}
}
```
As a test, if the header you received was *Basic bXlFbGF2b25XZWJob29rU2VydmljZTplM01uTiE/cGY0*, then the decryption code above should result in a username of *myElavonWebhookService* and a password of *e3MnN!?pf4*.
Even though the RFC7617 specification is HTTP, we require you to implement this security via an HTTPS connection. Your webhook URL cannot be registered unless it is an HTTPS endpoint.
3. A webhook with a cryptographic signature
When you receive a push notification, you can use a cryptographic signature to validate the data's integrity. A shared secret can be created at any time by contacting support. The shared secret will only be shared with you once, so it should be saved in a secure location. You will also receive a signer ID alongside this which should also be saved for future validations.
EPG uses this shared secret to sign notifications. These signed notifications will be present in the headers under `signer-`. You must ensure the value of the signer ID matches what support provided alongside their shared secret to ensure that the notification belongs to you. Each signer takes up its own header (i.e. support will provide five headers if five signers are requested).
```java
import org.apache.commons.lang3.ArrayUtils;
import java.nio.charset.StandardCharsets;
import java.security.MessageDigest;
import java.security.NoSuchAlgorithmException;
import java.util.Base64;
public class SecretGeneratorAndHasher
{
/**
* Function to generate the encrypted notification body
* @param notification - notificationBody we want to encrypt
* @param sharedSecret - secret from signer. acts like the salt
* @return the generated signature
*/
public static String signatureGenerator(String notification, String sharedSecret)
{
MessageDigest md;
try
{
md = MessageDigest.getInstance("SHA-512");
byte[] sharedSecretsBytes = Base64.getDecoder().decode(sharedSecret);
byte[] bodyBytes = notification.getBytes(StandardCharsets.UTF_8);
byte[] finalBytes = ArrayUtils.addAll(sharedSecretsBytes, bodyBytes);
byte[] bytes = md.digest(finalBytes);
return Base64.getEncoder().encodeToString(bytes);
}
catch (NoSuchAlgorithmException e)
{
e.printStackTrace();
throw new RuntimeException(e);
}
}
}
```
The code above generates the signed notification and takes in the deserialized notification body as a JSON string with the shared secret from support. The output of this should match the header's value.
Here are the steps to replicate this on your own:
1. Decode the *sharedSecret* string variable using Base64 and store that in an array of bytes.
2. Get the bytes from the notification string variable using the UTF_8 charset and store that in a different array of bytes.
3. Concatenate these two byte arrays together, ensuring that the *sharedSecret* byte array comes first.
4. Apply the SHA-512 hashing algorithm onto this concatenated byte array and store this result.
5. Finally, the output of step 4 can be encoded using Base64 to get the final result.
As an example, for a shared secret of:
```HTML
h/vw9kedkaXDZ2p/JCwi/VsHO4J3pBfJCQZkEPmfq0/PEYPKip/5uqsvY4LbdZxKdxE/Kp68LA5sMRhHJwRPvA==
```
And a notification body of:
```json
{
"href": "http://localhost:8443/notifications/42gvd623v6gjwj9pprmxm93vvkvb",
"id": "42gvd623v6gjwj9pprmxm93vvkvb",
"merchant": "http://localhost:8443/merchants/kff2tbyqbmqycycqbt48ythm89yx",
"createdAt": "2023-11-03T20:48:30.476Z",
"eventType": "saleAuthorized",
"resourceType": "transaction",
"resource": "http://localhost:8443/transactions/7qkxtkjpb23fpmfckjfpw2fxqphr"
}
```
The output should be:
```HTML
BYPhl69vk6S5A4lEc/asiPWZMz+gUiIsx7DopUWU79uXqqHhCBJ17u+I32iVdlmugEZxxv7kQbNXOVNkmYFHhQ==
```
This can act as a unit test.
4. A combination of #2 and #3
If you have chosen to have a username and password for your webhook in addition to any number of signers, you will receive both headers. This is the highest level of security offered when implemented over an HTTPS connection.
You will need to contact support to request a push notification. If you are an existing Elavon customer looking for help with your setup, please contact the [EPG helpdesk](overview#support).
## Sample Notifications
### Example: Transaction Authorized Notification
Below is a sample notification for a basic transaction case:
```json
{
"href": "https://api.eu.elavonpayments.com/notifications/j8t32fd7p29twgyj2fcb6dd4fdpw",
"id": "j8t32fd7p29twgyj2fcb6dd4fdpw",
"merchant": "https://api.eu.elavonpayments.com/merchants/3x7gwcy33vb84hb732pr44fvfkqh",
"createdAt": "2023-10-31T20:59:45.210Z",
"eventType": "saleAuthorized",
"resourceType": "transaction",
"resource": "https://api.eu.elavonpayments.com/transactions/ch7g7gmmxfgyvq7thkrm3ccdk3gy"
}
```
### Example: Payment Session and Payment Link Notifications
If a transaction has been created using a payment link, two notifications will be sent:
1. One notification for the authorization of the transaction:
```json
{
"href": "https://api.eu.elavonpayments.com/notifications/j8t32fd7p29twgyj2fcb6dd4fdpw",
"id": "j8t32fd7p29twgyj2fcb6dd4fdpw",
"merchant": "https://api.eu.elavonpayments.com/merchants/3x7gwcy33vb84hb732pr44fvfkqh",
"createdAt": "2023-10-31T20:59:45.210Z",
"eventType": "saleAuthorized",
"resourceType": "transaction",
"resource": "https://api.eu.elavonpayments.com/transactions/ch7g7gmmxfgyvq7thkrm3ccdk3gy"
}
```
2. Another notification regarding the payment link associated with this transaction:
```json
{
"href": "https://api.eu.elavonpayments.com/notifications/h6k2wpxbx26tf2cf72wd83cqk48w",
"id": "h6k2wpxbx26tf2cf72wd83cqk48w",
"merchant": "https://api.eu.elavonpayments.com/merchants/3x7gwcy33vb84hb732pr44fvfkqh",
"createdAt": "2023-10-31T20:59:45.220Z",
"eventType": "saleAuthorized",
"resourceType": "paymentLink",
"resource": "https://api.eu.elavonpayments.com/payment-links/8rcrym3w74kxwcg92xb47pv9rp3c"
}
```
To identify the transaction associated with this payment link notification, send a GET request and note the transaction resource ID in the JSON response:
```json
GET https://api.eu.elavonpayments.com/payment-links/8rcrym3w74kxwcg92xb47pv9rp3c
```
Similarly, a payment session notification for a basic transaction case will look like:
```json
{
"href": "https://api.na.elavonpayments.com/notifications/dp8hy679dkd64cv47pdb6x8jvchc",
"id": "dp8hy679dkd64cv47pdb6x8jvchc",
"merchant": "https://api.na.elavonpayments.com/merchants/3x7gwcy33vb84hb732pr44fvfkqh",
"createdAt": "2023-10-31T20:59:45.230Z",
"eventType": "saleAuthorized",
"resourceType": "paymentSession",
"resource": "https://api.na.elavonpayments.com/payment-sessions/9664jft37qqgwmmtjwrfkmr28v8x"
}
```
To identify the transaction associated with this payment session notification, send a GET request and note the transaction resource ID in the JSON response:
```json
GET https://api.na.elavonpayments.com/payment-sessions/9664jft37qqgwmmtjwrfkmr28v8x
```
If a payment link or payment session expires, a notification same as above will be sent, but with the eventType being *expired*. Note that *reset* only applies to payment session.
:::::notice{note}
Notifications for a transaction are sent regardless if it has been created with or without a payment link or a payment session.
If it has been created with a payment link and/or payment session, the payment link and/or payment session will only have notifications for eventTypes `saleAuthorized`, `saleDeclined`, and `saleAuthorizationPending`.
For example, `saleSettled` (and the rest of the eventTypes for transaction aside from the three mentioned above) will be sent in all cases for resourceType *transaction*, but not for payment sessions and payment links.
:::::

_source.title: Expand query parameter
_source.genAiContent:
# elavon-payment-gateway
## Using the Expand Query Parameter
Resources may contain fields that have fields within them. However, these nested fields are not shown by default when a resource is retrieved. For example, when retrieving a transaction's details, the response you get contains a *merchant* field, but the details of this field, such as its *id* and *legalName*, are not shown in that response.
If you want to retrieve these nested details, you need to add some parameters in the URL to expand the specified fields. The basic format of an expand query parameter is as follows:
`/{resource}/{id}?expand={field}`
In the example above, to expand the *merchant* field of a transaction, use the following URL:
`/transactions/{id}?expand=merchant`
The expand query parameter allows multiple fields separated by a comma (,). Alternatively, you can append another expand query parameter with an ampersand (&) in the URL. Below are the formats:
`{resource}/{id}?expand={field1},{field2}`
`{resource}/{id}?expand={field1}&expand={field2}`
For example, if you want to retrieve the details of a transaction's *merchant* and *shopper*, you can use any of these URLs:
`/transactions/{id}?expand=merchant,shopper`
`/transactions/{id}?expand=merchant&expand=shopper`
For resources that have fields with additional nested layers, you can use a dot (.) to expand those fields as well. The format is as follows:
`/{resource}/{id}?expand={field1.field2}`
You can have a combination of parameters with different layers. Take a look at the example below:
`/transactions/{id}?expand=merchant,account.planList,shopper`
Note that the expand query parameter is only supported on GET calls. Using this parameter on other API calls will not have any effect.
In addition, not all fields can be expanded. Fields that can be used as part of the expand query parameter are those that can be retrieved by themselves via GET. In the examples above, *merchant*, *shopper*, *account*, and *planList* are all fields that are also resources that you can retrieve by themselves. A transaction's *type* is something that cannot be expanded because the *type* field is not something that you can retrieve by itself.
Here is a list of fields that can be expanded: merchant, processorAccount, account, planList, order, paymentLink, paymentLinkEvent, paymentSession, hostedCard, forexAdvice, transaction, batch, shopper, storedCard, plan, subscription, notification. Note that this is not a comprehensive list and that it only serves as a quick guide.

_source.title: rawProcessorResponseInfo
_source.genAiContent:
# elavon-payment-gateway
## rawProcessorResponseInfo
The **`rawProcessorResponseInfo`** query parameter allows API clients to view additional authorization details returned directly from the payment processor in Transaction and Total Adjustment responses. When enabled, the API response may include processor‑supplied values such as the authorization code, message, and address verification response. These values are passed through exactly as provided by the processor and are intended only for informational and diagnostic purposes.
:::::notice{Important}
- This parameter is intended for informational and diagnostic purposes only.
- Integrations should not make business or application decisions based on these values.
- EPG does not normalize or control processor‑returned codes or messages.
- Processor data may differ across card brands, processors, or transaction types.
- Processor‑returned values may change at any time without notice.
- Not all transactions will return all fields.
:::::
## Parameters
When `rawProcessorResponseInfo=true`, the response may include the following object:
| Parameter
|
Type
| Description |
|----------|-------------|:------|
| `rawProcessorResponseInfo` | object | A collection of additional processor‑returned authorization details. All fields are returned exactly as provided by the processor. |
## Properties
| Parameter
|
Type
| Description |
|----------|-------------|:------|
| `processorResponseCode` | string | the authorization response code returned by the processor. |
| `processorResponseMessage` | string | the corresponding processor‑supplied message. |
| `addressVerificationResponseCode` | string | the address verification (AVS) response returned by the processor. |
::::notice{Note}
These fields appear only when the processor provides the values.
:::::
This parameter applies to responses for both Transaction and Total Adjustment resources.
It may return processor‑level information in the following response types:
- **Transaction responses** (e.g., Create Transaction, Retrieve Transaction)
-
**Total Adjustment responses**
## Syntax : Retrieve Transaction
```HTTP
GET /transactions/{id}?rawProcessorResponseInfo=true
```
## Example usage
Retrieve Transaction with `rawProcessorResponseInfo`
:::code-block
```Shell
curl --request POST \
--url 'https://api.sandbox.elavonpayments.com/transactions?rawProcessorResponseInfo=true' \
--header 'Authorization: Basic {credentials}' \
--header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
--data '{
"total": {
"amount": "20.00",
"currencyCode": "EUR"
},
"card": {
"holderName": "Ela Von",
"number": "4485.5758.8674.8612",
"expirationMonth": 12,
"expirationYear": 2030
},
"shopperInteraction": "telephoneOrder",
"doCapture": false
}
'
```
:::
The `rawProcessorResponseInfo` object below contains unmodified processor‑returned values.
:::code-block
```json
{
"href": "https://api.sandbox.elavonpayments.com/transactions/tyym8fmhtwggffrph6xy79tc7qgm",
"id": "tyym8fmhtwggffrph6xy79tc7qgm",
```

:::
## Syntax : Total Adjustment
```HTTP

```
## Example usage


_source.title: Suppressing nulls
_source.genAiContent:
# elavon-payment-gateway
## Suppressing nulls
The `suppressNullFields` query parameter allows you to exclude fields with null values from API responses.
By default, all fields are returned regardless of their value. When this parameter is enabled, only fields with non‑null values are included in the response.
`suppressNullFields` is a **global query parameter supported on all API endpoints** that return a JSON response body.
This parameter can be appended to any request where a JSON response is returned, regardless of resource type or API domain.
**Purpose**
Use suppressNullFields to:
- Reduce response payload size
- Improve performance of bandwidth-sensitive applications
- Simplify client-side parsing when null values are not needed
**Considerations**
-
Integrations must handle of missing fields, not just fields with null values.
:::::notice{Note}
The parameter affects only the response format. It does not alter business logic, validation, or data availability.
:::::
## Parameters
| Parameter
|
Type
|
Default
| Description |
|----------|-------------|-------------|:------|
| `suppressNullFields` | `boolean` | `false` |When `true`, fields with `null` values are excluded from the API response. When `false` or not provided, all fields are returned, including those with `null` values.
|
## Syntax
```HTTP
?suppressNullFields=true
```
## Example request (default behavior)
```HTTP
GET /orders/9bgdygqyx6vmcxvyt83xdgcfkw2d HTTP/1.1
Authorization: Basic {credentials}
Host: api.sandbox.elavonpayments.com
```
## Example usage - response
API response where fields with `null` values are included by default when `suppressNullFields` is not applied.
:::code-block
```json
{
"href": "https://api.sandbox.elavonpayments.com/orders/9bgdygqyx6vmcxvyt83xdgcfkw2d",
"id": "9bgdygqyx6vmcxvyt83xdgcfkw2d",
"createdAt": "2026-03-02T20:21:33.597Z",
"modifiedAt": "2026-03-02T20:21:33.597Z",
"merchant": "https://api.sandbox.elavonpayments.com/merchants/XrDXRBh9YHxwqQTj2Cmq7j49",
"total":
{
"amount": "1.23",
"currencyCode": "USD"
}
,
"description": null,
"items": [
],
"shipTo": null,
"shopperEmailAddress": null,
"shopperReference": null,
"orderReference": null,
"customReference": null,
"customFields": null
}
```
:::
## Example request
Using
`suppressNullFields`, to exclude fields with `null` values, append the query parameter:
```http
GET /orders/72792j67j9f3gygtp6d72bpkv244?suppressNullFields=true HTTP/1.1
Authorization: Basic {credentials}
Host: api.sandbox.elavonpayments.com
```
## Example usage - response
The `null` valued fields that were previously included are now suppressed.
:::code-block
```json
{
"href": "https://api.sandbox.elavonpayments.com/orders/72792j67j9f3gygtp6d72bpkv244",
"id": "72792j67j9f3gygtp6d72bpkv244",
"createdAt": "2026-03-02T20:28:31.740Z",
"modifiedAt": "2026-03-02T20:28:31.740Z",
"merchant": "https://api.sandbox.elavonpayments.com/merchants/XrDXRBh9YHxwqQTj2Cmq7j49",
"total":
{
"amount": "1.23",
"currencyCode": "USD"
}
,
"items": []
}
```
:::

_source.title: Dynamic currency conversion
_source.genAiContent:
# elavon-payment-gateway
## Dynamic currency conversion compliance
Dynamic Currency Conversion (DCC) is a service which gives international customers using Mastercard, VISA or Discover the choice to pay in their home currency. The card brands have specific rules around the information that needs to be presented to the customer before they make their choice of whether to accept or decline DCC and what information needs to be present on the customer receipt.
**EU legislation requirements**
The EU legislation states that any DCC transaction where the card is issued in the European Economic Area (EEA) and the merchant is EEA-based, then the mark-up will be the % over the European Central Bank (ECB) rate. Any DCC transaction that doesn't meet both of these criteria is considered non-EEA and existing mark-up rules still apply in relation to screens and receipts.
:::::notice{note}
For Elavon Payment Gateway, the countries that fall into EEA scenarios include Poland and United Kingdom.
:::::
## Card-present payment screen
For point of sale transactions, there are two important card brand rules:
1. A customer must be shown how to accept or reject DCC, so it must be clear what buttons on the terminal or PIN pad to press.
2. A customer may not accept or reject DCC by selecting a 'green/red' button as the only options.
### How to build a compliant DCC customer screen
The screen must show:
- Local amount and currency
- DCC amount and currency
- Exchange rate
- Mark-up % must be displayed directly underneath the exchange rate line
- A clear instruction, for example, "Select currency"
- A clear way for the customer to either accept or decline DCC
- Make sure all text is the same size on the terminal screens
:::collapsibleContent
Tips for customer-facing screens
| Tip | EEA currency example | Non-EEA currency example |
|--------------------------------|--------------------------|--------------------|
| **If terminal supports colour**
If the terminal is a touch screen, then we recommend that the selection can be made on screen by touching a currency flag.
Adding currency flags to a touch screen can optimise DCC take-up rates. | :image{id="/content/dam/developer-portal-solutions/en-us/elavon/media/images/products/elavon-payment-gateway/eea-currency-flag.png" alt="EEA currency with country flags shown as selection options" max-width="" max-height=""} | :image{id="/content/dam/developer-portal-solutions/en-us/elavon/media/images/products/elavon-payment-gateway/non-eea-currency-flag.png" alt="Non-EEA currency with country flags shown as selection options" max-width="" max-height=""} |
| **If terminal has buttons under the currency selection screen:**
The buttons should be used to allow the customer to make their selection | :image{id="/content/dam/developer-portal-solutions/en-us/elavon/media/images/products/elavon-payment-gateway/eea-currency-buttons.png" alt="EEA currency with buttons shown as selection options" max-width="" max-height=""} | :image{id="/content/dam/developer-portal-solutions/en-us/elavon/media/images/products/elavon-payment-gateway/non-eea-currency-buttons.png" alt="Non-EEA currency with buttons shown as selection options" max-width="" max-height=""} |
| **If terminal has no buttons under the currency selection screen:**
We recommend using number buttons 1 and 2 (1 to decline and 2 to accept) | :image{id="/content/dam/developer-portal-solutions/en-us/elavon/media/images/products/elavon-payment-gateway/eea-currency-no-buttons.png" alt="EEA currency with 1 or 2 as selection options" max-width="" max-height=""} | :image{id="/content/dam/developer-portal-solutions/en-us/elavon/media/images/products/elavon-payment-gateway/non-eea-currency-no-buttons.png" alt="Non-EEA currency with 1 or 2 shown as selection options" max-width="" max-height=""} |
:::
## Receipt requirements
**Sale:**
- Local amount and currency
- Exchange rate
- Mark-up %: must be displayed on the line under the exchange rate. Rate above European Central Bank for EEA currencies.
- DCC amount and currency, followed by the words, "Transaction currency."
- Disclaimer as follows: "I have been offered a choice of currencies and have chosen to accept DCC and pay in at today's exchange rate."
- Provider has 2 options:
- "DCC provided by Elavon Merchant Services. Elavon receive Foreign Exchange Transaction Services from U.S. Bank." **OR**
- "Dynamic currency conversion (DCC) is offered by merchant."
**Refunds:**
- If the solution is DCC enabled, it must support DDC refunds.
- A DCC refund should use the live rate available on the day the refund is processed.
- Refund receipts must include all the information required on a DCC sale receipt.
**Express check-out:**
By having a disclaimer on pre-authorisation receipts, you can complete the check-out with DCC if the customer is not present at time of check-out and they have opted-in to DCC at the check-in stage.
- It is required that you have a tick box at the start of the disclaimer on the pre-authorisation receipt which will be auto-ticked if the customer selected DCC at pre-authorisation.
Disclaimer text for pre-authorisation receipts:
[X] In the event that I am not present at check-out, I accept that any final amount/transaction currency owed will be settled in the same currency and that the currency conversion will be performed using the rate in force on that date without further consultation with myself.
## Online and card not present payment screens
For Mastercard ecommerce and online transactions, when point-of-interaction (POI) currency conversion is offered on the internet, a currency conversion option may be pre-selected. When POI currency conversion is offered for an ecommerce transaction and the currency conversion option is pre-selected, the cardholder must be informed of the pre-selection and provided with the means to decline the currency conversion.
For VISA transactions, when a DCC payment is made online or when the card is not present, all information relating to the DCC transaction must be displayed on your website or provided to the customer before the authorisation takes place.
### Mandatory customer-facing DCC information
Payment screens must show:
- A clear instruction, for example, "Select currency"
- DCC amount and currency
- Exchange rate
- Mark-up %: EEA transactions need to show a percentage above ECB rate, such as 3.72% over ECB rate. Non-EEA transactions must display the mark-up as a percentage, such as 3.5%.
- A clear way for the customer to either accept or decline DCC
- Local amount and currency
- Disclaimer: MAKE SURE YOU UNDERSTAND THE COSTS OF CURRENCY CONVERSION AS THEY MAY BE DIFFERENT DEPENDING ON WHETHER YOU SELECT YOUR HOME CURRENCY OR THE TRANSACTION CURRENCY.
- Provider has 2 options:
- "DCC provided by Elavon Merchant Services. Elavon receive Foreign Exchange Transaction Services from U.S. Bank." **OR**
- "Dynamic currency conversion (DCC) is offered by merchant."
**Recurring transactions**
To process DCC on recurring transactions, the customer must agree to accept DCC for subsequent transactions by opting in at the time of the first transaction. Relevant financial information must be provided to the consumer for the first transaction and be available for all subsequent transactions. A process must be available for the consumer to change their mind and choose not to accept DCC for subsequent transactions.
**Refunds**
- A refund transaction must be processed in the same currency used for the original purchase.
- A DCC refund should use the live rate available on the day the refund is processed.
**Receipt requirements**
- If the customer accepts DCC and confirms their acceptance by checking a box, all transaction receipt statements should then be displayed on the screen.
- When a customer accepts DCC and the transaction completes, the customer must receive a transaction receipt (for example, by either email or post) in line with the DCC transaction receipt requirements which are both amounts, both currencies, exchange rate, and mark-up %.
## European Economic Area (EEA) countries
From a DCC perspective, countries that fall into EEA scenarios include:
- Bulgaria
- Czechia
- Denmark
- Euro Zone
- Hungary
- Iceland
- Liechtenstein
- Norway
- Poland
- Romania
- Sweden
- UK

_source.title: Release Notes
_source.genAiContent:
# elavon-payment-gateway
## Release notes
:::::notice{Note}
This Release Notes page covers content for both North America (US) and Europe (EU). Features and updates that are specific to a single region are clearly labeled where applicable.
:::::
## April 2026
**New features**
*
In‑person transactions (Applies to EU only)
* Terminal support (terminal availability Q2 2026).
* Restaurant and lodging market segment support.
* Adjustable transaction support.
* Partial approval support.
* Standalone refunds.
* China UnionPay support.
*
Reusable payment links
* Use limits (Unlimited or defined max uses).
* Merchant-entered and shopper-entered amounts.
* Ability to label the order reference field.
*
Apple pay browser compatibility expanded.
**API changes**
- Updated payment link and payment session to support for creating and managing reusable payment links.
- New API for retrieving exchange rate tables.
- New API for retrieving supported currencies.
- Added DCC support for partially authorizable transactions.
- Credential on file transactions now support installments. (Applies to EU only)
- Ability to import stored cards without verification.
- Security code is optional when verification is disabled.
**Merchant portal UI changes**
- New consolidated Payment Links Table with enhanced filtering and visibility.
- Enhanced reusable link transaction drawer.
- CSV export updated with link-type, usage, and limits fields.
- Updates to fraud review section in transaction details drawer to clarify 3D secure results.
**Hosted payments page changes**
- Added support for user‑entered amounts and order reference.
**Bug fixes**
- Resolved an issue causing Back Office UI login sessions to time out prematurely.
**General changes**
- Support for recurring payments to properly determine subscription or installment credential on file type (Applies only in EU)
- General performance, security, and reliability enhancements.
## January 2026
**New features**
- Added support for lodging merchants. (Change only applies in US)
- Added Apple Tap to Pay support. (Change only applies in US)
**API changes**
- Added DCC support for adjustable sale transactions.
(Change only applies in US)
- Added support for validating card details with card issuer. See [Card Verification](api-reference#tag/Card-Verifications) API resource.
**Hosted payments page changes**
- Added DCC support for adjustable sale transactions.
(Change only applies in US)
- Added support for :magic-link[stored cards]{id="stored-shoppers-and-cards"}
- Added ability to perform logic before transaction submission in :magic-link[Hosted Payment Fields]{id="hosted-payments-overview#hosted-fields"}.
**Bug fixes**
- No customer-reported bug fixes included in this release.
**General changes**
- Added security, performance, and reliability enhancements.
- Added accessibility improvements for assistive technologies.
## October 2025
**API changes**
- Added ability to capture transactions in a batch and submit together for settlement. See [Manual Batch](api-reference#tag/Manual-Batches) API resource.
- Updated surcharging capabilities for Subscriptions and digital wallet payments (Apple Pay, Google Pay, and Paze).
**Back Office UI changes**
- Updated support email information.
**Hosted payments page changes**
- Added `updateSession` function and improved surcharge submission in [Hosted Payment Fields](hosted-payment-fields).
**Bug fixes**
- No customer-reported bug fixes included in this release.
**General Changes**
- Added security, performance, and reliability enhancements.
- Added accessibility improvements for assistive technologies.
## July 2025
**New features**
- Added support for restaurant merchants.
**API changes**
- Added ability to adjust sale transaction amount prior to pre-authorization completion.
- PIN entry can now be skipped for in-person debit transactions for amounts less than a certain threshold (varies with card brand).
- Added ACH payment support.
- Authorization expirations now follow card brand guidelines more closely, based on details that may vary for each transaction.
- Added payment link notifications.
**Back Office UI changes**
- Improved transaction drawer layout and design.
- Added ACH payment support.
- Tip amount is now displayed.
**Hosted payments page changes**
- Added ACH payment support.
- Changed messageType *hostedCardCreated* to *sessionCompleted* when doCreateTransaction is set to false
in [HPP Lightbox](hosted-payments-lightbox).
**Bug fixes**
- No customer-reported bug fixes included in this release.
**General changes**
- Added security, performance, and reliability enhancements.
- Added accessibility improvements for assistive technologies.
## May 2025
**API changes**
- Added provision and last transaction timestamps for terminals.
- Payment link description is replicated to transaction description.
**Hosted payments page changes**
- Improved email field behavior for customer convenience.
**Bug fixes**
- No customer-reported bug fixes included in this release.
**General changes**
- Added security, performance, and reliability enhancements.
- Added accessibility improvements for assistive technologies.
## March 2025
**New features**
- Added ability to securely capture a payment method
for use in future transactions, with or without requiring a transaction.
**API changes**
- Added ability to list Merchants resource collection.
- Added health check API endpoint.
- Transactions can now accept an invoice number and shipping date which will be passed through to the processor.
- Transactions now reflect the processor batch reference after settlement.
- Payment links now have a status to more easily track their life cycle.
- Payment sessions now include a URL that can be provided to shoppers to access our HPP.
- Removed 3DS V1 functionality because it is no longer supported by the card networks.
**Back Office UI changes**
- Added ability to send transaction receipts to multiple BCC recipients.
- Updated cookie policy: removed non-essential cookies and enabled necessary cookies by default.
**Hosted payments page changes**
- Added Paze Payment support in Hosted Payment Fields.
**Bug fixes**
- Description is properly saved on payment links.
**General changes**
- Added security, performance, and reliability enhancements.
- Added accessibility improvements for assistive technologies.
## 2024
:::collapsibleContent
November 2024
**New features**
- Added support for in-person payments.
- Added support for Paze Wallet in HPP.
- Added support for China UnionPay cards.
- Added Hosted Payment Fields integration option.
**API changes**
- A payment session may be reused until at most 7 declined transactions.
- Card payments can now be optionally partially authorized.
**Back Office UI changes**
- Increased password length requirement to 12 characters. Users with passwords not meeting the requirement will be asked to update their passwords.
- Users who have not logged in for more than 90 days will be deactivated.
- Users list can now be downloaded in CSV format.
- CSV transaction download now includes new fields, including in-person fields and the transaction ID.
**Hosted payments page changes**
- HPP users can attempt to provide a successful payment method multiple times with a single payment session.
- Apple Pay is now supported in HPP lightbox integration.
**Bug fixes**
- No customer-reported bug fixes included in this release.
**General changes**
- Added security, performance, and reliability enhancements.
- Added accessibility improvements for assistive technologies.
:::collapsibleContent
August 2024
**API changes**
- When the ThreeDSecure protocolVersion is not provided, it now defaults to "2.2.0" instead of "2.1.0".
- Transaction advice can provide additional information about a transaction from the host.
- Added support for sending email confirmations to customers upon creation, update, and cancellation of a subscription when doSendConfirmationEmails is true.
- Transaction resource now has a totalRefundable money amount field that indicates how much is left to be refunded.
- Payment session now has a createdBy field which will be mapped into the transaction for HPP transactions.
**Back Office UI changes**
- Pay-By-Link filters now allow selecting multiple statuses to search for.
- Removed required fields disclaimer from pages with no required fields.
- Fraud Rule Settings - Restructured Fraud Prevention Rules settings page to help better identify which rules apply to different payment types.
- Added support for sending email confirmations to customers upon creation, update, and cancellation of a subscription. This feature is only available in North America.
- Added cookie banner button for declining all non-essential cookies by default.
**Hosted payments page changes**
- Added 3DS v2.2 support.
**Bug fixes**
- No customer-reported bug fixes included in this release.
**General changes**
- Added security, performance, and reliability enhancements.
- Added accessibility improvements for assistive technologies.
:::collapsibleContent
June 2024
**API changes**
- New resources:
- Surcharge Advice - A surcharge advice calculates the amount of surcharge and provides an adjusted transaction total for specific card numbers.
- Refund Surcharge Advice - A refund surcharge advice calculates the amount of refundable surcharge and provides an adjusted refund transaction amount using the previous surcharge advice and the original parent transaction.
- New fields:
- Added surchargeAdvice to the Payment Session resource to support the Surcharge Advice resource.
- Added surchargeAdvice and refundSurchargeAdvice to the Transaction resource to support the Surcharge Advice and Refund Surcharge Advice resources.
- New type:
- Surcharge - Surcharge information if surchargeAdvice or refundSurchargeAdvice was created for the transaction.
**Back Office UI changes**
- Navigation - Updated the layout of the left-hand menu items. The Account selector has been moved out of the left navigation menu and into the main page content. It will only appear on pages it is relevant to.
- Surcharging - Added the ability to add surcharges to transactions. This also applies to hosted payments pages. It is only available to NA merchants and it must be enabled through boarding systems.
- Password reset - Resetting the password no longer requires the user to enter the old and new passwords. The user is now sent a link to their email when resetting their password where they will be asked to enter and confirm their new password.
- Hosted payments page and pay-by-link settings - Email related settings for hosted payments page and pay-by-link have been consolidated. The user can now configure the email settings for these at the Hosted Payments Page Settings.
- Payment settings - Added the optional Description field that allows users to add notes to transactions at the Create Transaction page.
- Dynamic currency conversion - Updated the DCC disclosure information notice at the Create Transaction page, the presentation of the currency selection, and the DCC statement in the popup confirmation dialog.
**Hosted payments page changes**
- The Transaction Complete screen is no longer shown to the user except for pay-by-link transactions. The user is redirected to the return URL instead.
- Added an email field to meet new 3DS requirements. This field is required if you are using a payment method that supports 3DS.
**Bug fixes**
- Hosted payments page preview now translates to Polish if this language is selected.
**General changes**
- Added security, performance, and reliability enhancements.
- Added accessibility improvements for assistive technologies.
:::collapsibleContent
March 2024
**Enhancements**
- HPP enhancements - If HPP is configured to require a shipping address and a payment session with a shopper is used, the shopper's shipping address, if available, will now be prepopulated.
- Hosted card enhancements:
- Forex advice can now be created from a hosted card.
- Hosted cards no longer expire after their first use (e.g. creating a stored card). They now expire either after they have been used in transactions or after 30 minutes if unused.
- Account shopper statements - Shopper statement functionality determines how a transaction will appear on cardholder statements received from their banks. When no shopper statement is explicitly provided for a transaction, we will now default to the information specified on the account rather than the information received from the boarding system. The information specified on the account can be edited by the merchant in the Back Office UI.
**API changes**
- Added *logoUrl* to the :magic-link[Accounts]{id="converge-api-reference#tag/Accounts"} resource to identify the URL of the merchant's logo.
- Added *hostedCard* to the :magic-link[Forex Advices]{id="converge-api-reference#tag/Forex-Advices"} resource to allow hosted card support.
- Added *allowedPaymentMethods* and *allowedPaymentMethodOrigins* to the :magic-link[Payment Sessions]{id="converge-api-reference#tag/Payment-Sessions"} resource to specify the payment methods and origins allowed to be shown in the hosted payments page.
- For backwards compatibility, when *doCreateTransaction* is false, the allowed payment methods default to "Card" because the integrator might be unprepared to create a transaction themselves with new payment methods. When *doCreateTransaction* is true, we default to everything supported for the merchant. Integrators can override the default if their integration can handle new payment methods.
**Back Office UI changes**
- User notification service - Announcements are now notified to users through the bell icon at the upper right corner of the Back Office UI. Clicking the icon opens a list of notices.
- Editing stored cards - Stored cards can now be edited from the Edit Subscription page.
- Password enhancements - Passwords no longer expire. Users are no longer required to log in immediately after setting their passwords.
- Account settings validation - Updated the maximum field length of the following account settings:
- Account Name - 300
- Company name - 300
- Company email - 300
- Address Line 1 - 300
- Address Line 2 - 300
- Postal Code - 50
**Bug fixes**
- Users logged in to the Back Office UI with multiple tabs now remain logged in on those tabs as long as they are active on at least one of them.
**General changes**
- Added security, performance, and reliability enhancements.
- Added accessibility improvements for assistive technologies.
**Known issues**
- Plus signs (+) are not working on transaction searches at the Back Office UI.

_source.title: Error Codes
_source.genAiContent:
# elavon-payment-gateway
## Error codes
## Failed API Calls
For any
unsuccessful API responses (having a non-2xx HTTP status), a JSON error response will be returned with the following fields:
- "status": number that simply duplicates the HTTP status code
- "failures" object array where each failure contains the following fields:
- "code": enumerated string value that categorizes the failure
- "description": string value with more details about the specific problem.
- "field": string value that identifies a field in the JSON request, where applicable
In some cases, the *"code"* identifies a general category of failures that may have many descriptions. Below is an example:
Request:
```json
POST "https://uat.api.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/orders"
{
"total": {
"amount": "1.23"
}
}
```
Response:
```json
{
"status": 400,
"failures": [
{
"code": "badRequest",
"description": "The request is invalid; correct all issues before resending",
"field": null
},
{
"code": "fieldValidationFailure",
"description": "must not be null",
"field": "total.currencyCode"
}
]
}
```
### HTTP failure codes
Generally, the first failure will provide more information related to the HTTP status code. The table below shows a list of the HTTP status codes and their descriptions:
| HTTP
Status | Failure Code | Failure Description |
|---|---|---|
| 400 | badRequest | The request is invalid; correct all issues before resending |
| 401 | unauthorized | A valid API key is required |
| 403 | forbidden | A secret API key must be used |
| 404 | notFound | The requested resource does not exist |
| 405 | methodNotAllowed | The given method may not be used here. Note that the X-HTTP-Method-Override header can only be used to override POST with either PATCH or DELETE |
| 406 | notAcceptable | The Accept request header must specify 'application/json;charset=UTF-8'; the Accept-Version request header if present, must reference a valid API version |
| 409 | conflict | This request does not match the original, or the original request is still processing |
| 411 | lengthRequired | Content-Length must be specified for this request |
| 413 | payloadTooLarge | The request payload is too large |
| 415 | unsupportedMediaType | The Content-Type request header must specify 'application/json' |
| 429 | tooManyRequests | Too many requests have been received too quickly for this API key |
| 500 | internalServerError | Internal server error |
Additional failures will describe as many additional specific issues as can be currently identified. Because descriptions are sometimes generated dynamically, it's impossible to enumerate all possibilities.
### jsonBindingFailure
The JSON request is parsed and bound to a resource as defined by the API specification. Failures at this stage of processing will have the code *"jsonBindingFailure"*. Descriptions specify as much as possible to help identify the issue encountered.
Below are examples of these types of failures. Some details of each description (e.g. field names, line and column numbers) will vary.
| Failure Code | Failure Description |
|---|---|
| jsonBindingFailure | Unexpected character ('c' (code 99)): was expecting double-quote to start field name; near line: 2, column 4 |
| jsonBindingFailure | Unexpected character ('}' (code 125)): expected a value; near line: 3, column 2 |
| jsonBindingFailure | Illegal unquoted character ((CTRL-CHAR, code 13)): has to be escaped using backslash to be included in string value; near line: 2, column 15 |
| jsonBindingFailure | Unrecognized token 'value': expected ('true', 'false', or 'null') near line: 3, column 26 |
| jsonBindingFailure | Can not construct name enum from 'invalidValue' |
| jsonBindingFailure | Can not construct name enum from empty string |
| jsonBindingFailure | Can not construct name enum from boolean |

| jsonBindingFailure | Can not deserialize 'fieldName' from String |

| jsonBindingFailure | Can not deserialize 'fieldName' from String: not a valid Integer value |
| jsonBindingFailure | Can not deserialize 'fieldName' from START_ARRAY token ('[') |
| jsonBindingFailure | Can not deserialize 'fieldName' from START_OBJECT token ('{') |
| jsonBindingFailure | Can not deserialize 'fieldName' from VALUE_FALSE |
| jsonBindingFailure | Can not deserialize 'fieldName' from VALUE_NUMBER_FLOAT |

### Filter failures
If query parameters are provided to perform filtering, validation will also be applied to those.
Below are examples of these types of failures. The specific values for the field name and operator will vary.
| Failure Code | Failure Description |
|---|---|
| filtersNotSupported | filters are not supported by this resource |
| filterParseFailure | Invalid value for fieldName 'fieldName' |
| filterParseFailure | Unrecognized operator: 'op' |
| filterParseFailure | Unrecognized operator 'op' for fieldName 'fieldName' |
For more information on filtering, see :magic-link[API Reference > Collection Filtering]{id="api-reference#section/Overview/Collection-Filtering"}.
## Declined Transactions
When creating a transaction, if the request is valid, an authorization is performed for the transaction. If the transaction authorization is declined, a transaction is still created and a successful status code is returned. To determine why the transaction was declined, check the *"failures"* object array in the created transaction.
The first failure identifies where in the authorization chain the transaction was declined. Below is a list of these types of failures.
| Failure Code | Failure Description |
|---|---|
| declinedByGateway | Transaction was declined by the payment gateway |
| declinedByProcessor | Declined by the payment processor |
| declinedByIssuer | Declined by the card issuer |
| declinedByIssuer | Declined by the issuer |
There may be additional failures that provide more information about why the transaction was declined. Below is a list of these types of failures.
| Failure Code | Failure Description |
|---|---|
| 3dsEnforcedOnEcommerceSales | Declined because 3DS shopper authentication was not performed or failed |
| avsPostalCodeMismatch | Declined because AVS result is postal code mismatch |
| avsStreetMismatch | Declined because AVS result is street mismatch |
| cannotVerifyPin | Unable to verify PIN |
| cardExpired | Declined because the card has expired |
| cardNotValidForCredentialOnFile | Card is not valid for credential on file transaction |
| cardTypeNotSupported | Declined because the card type is not supported by merchant |
| cvvMismatch | Declined because the CVV does not match |
| doNotResubmit | Resubmission will be declined and may incur additional fees |
| highRiskBillingCountry | Declined because the billing country is rated as high-risk |
| highRiskIpAddress | Declined because it was generated from an IP address rated as high-risk |
| highRiskIssuingCountry | Declined because the card issuing country is rated as high-risk |
| highRiskPan | Declined because the card PAN was recognized as a high-risk PAN |
| insufficientFunds | Declined because of insufficient funds |
| invalidPin | Invalid PIN |
| limitExceeded | Declined because of too many authorization attempts |
| maestro3dsRequired | 3DS must be performed on Maestro E-commerce transactions |
| maxAmountExceeded | Declined because it exceeds the maximum amount allowed |
| merchantNotActive | Declined because the merchant is currently inactive |
| missingBinData | Declined because BIN file service did not return any data |
| panFrequencyCheck | Declined because card pan was seen too often in defined time frame |
| paymentLinkLimitReached | The conversion limit for this payment link has been reached |
| pinEntryLimit | Exceeds allowable PIN entry attempts |
| processorError | The processor is unavailable or an error occurred |
| timeout | Declined because of confirmation timeout |
| userDeclined | Declined because the user declined the transaction |
| userTimeout | Declined because the transaction timed out waiting for user confirmation |
Below is an example of a valid transaction that was declined. The *"failures"* object array in the response indicates why the transaction was declined.
Request:
```json
POST "https://uat.api.converge.eu.elavonaws.com/transactions"
{
"total":
{
"amount":
"10.00",
"currencyCode":
"EUR"
},
"card":
{
"number":
"5021652222222228",
"expirationMonth":
1,
"expirationYear":
2025
},
"shopperInteraction": "telephoneOrder"
}
```
Response:

_source.title: Testing
_source.genAiContent:
# elavon-payment-gateway
## Testing
Use the card numbers below when testing your integration. These are all test cards approved for use with Elavon's test systems and they will decline if used in live environments. Also, using any other test card numbers not listed here may result in a decline.
:::::notice{warning}
Using live card numbers for testing is prohibited.
:::::
:::::notice{note}
To ensure approvals, use whole amounts (e.g., 1.00).
:::::
| Card Type | Card Number |
|---|---|
| Visa Credit | 4546341111111119 |
| Visa Corporate | 4159288888888882 |
| Visa Debit | 4539785000002517 |
| MasterCard Credit | 5432673003275469 |
| MasterCard Debit | 5108406713369360 |
| MasterCard Maestro | 6772555678901238 |
| AMEX | 341382706525920 |
| Diners | 36252960520018
6011000000000004 |
| JCB | 3566664444444445 |
For the expiration date, you can use any future date.
For the Card Security Code (CSC), you can use the following:
- Any three digits for Visa, MC, Discover (e.g. 123, 321, 748, etc.)
- Any four digits for American Express (e.g. 1234, 8590, etc.)
The Card Security Code (CSC) is sometimes called CVV, CVV2, CVC, or CVC2. For AMEX, this is the card identification number (CID).
## Error Messages
There are three types of error messages:
- Declined by Gateway - transaction was declined by Elavon Payment Gateway.
- Declined by Issuer - transaction was declined by the cardholder's issuing bank.
- Declined by Processor - transaction was declined by the acquirer.
```json
"failures" : [
{
"code" : "declinedByProcessor",
"description" : "Transaction was declined by the payment processor",
"field" : null
},
{
"code" : "cardExpired",
"description" : "Transaction was declined because the card has expired",
"field" : null
}
]
```
## Generating Error Messages
To generate a specific error message, use the following card numbers:
| Card Number | Error Type |
|---|---|
| 4380564306105898 | Declined by Issuer |
| 4539357499250369 | Declined by Processor |
To generate a declined by gateway error message, create a transaction that will go against a fraud rule that you have set. For example, if the maximum transaction amount for a fraud rule is set to $100, create a transaction greater than that amount.
To generate an expired card error message, use a month and year combination that represents an expiration date in the past.

_source.title: FAQs
_source.genAiContent:
# elavon-payment-gateway
## FAQs
**What is Elavon Payment Gateway?**
Elavon Payment Gateway is a new product offering for our customers. It is a solution built in-house that allows e-commerce and MOTO customers to accept card payments in their business.
The product offering comes with three integration methods:
- **Hosted Payments Page (HPP)** – HPP is the simplest integration option and can be configured through the merchant’s back office. It is for e-commerce customers who don’t want to handle card details themselves as the HPP is hosted on our servers, which means cardholder information is collected securely and not stored on the customer’s server.
- **Lightbox** – This works with our HPP. It uses JavaScript to display a pop-up window on the customer’s website instead of redirecting the cardholder to a browser payment form.
- **REST APIs** – This is a list of APIs made available for direct integration. APIs use JSON to send and receive data. This option requires more development effort and would be aimed towards larger customers or partners capable of handling and securing cardholder information on their servers.
**What is a hosted payments page?**
A hosted payments page is a page hosted outside your website that can accept user payments. Because the page is hosted outside your website, you are able to qualify for lower Payment Card Industry compliance standards and avoid costly development and testing requirements.
**What are the requirements for a hosted payments page?**
To host a website that contains a hosted payments page, your server will need to meet a set of minimum security standards, including running on the HTTPS protocol and using TLS version 1.2 or greater.
Shoppers accessing the hosted payments page will need to use a supported browser. The hosted payments page is tested and confirmed to run on the recent version and one version prior of Chrome and Firefox.
The hosted payments page does not run on Internet Explorer or Microsoft Edge.
**Why am I charged with a fee for resubmitting a declined Mastercard transaction?**
To tackle issues concerning declined transactions, Mastercard introduced its Merchant Advice Code Transaction Processing Excellence Program. This program is aimed more specifically at stopping resubmissions of transactions on closed or fraudulent cardholder accounts.
The Mastercard program applies to card-not-present (CNP) transactions only. If a CNP transaction is submitted and then declined with a MAC code of 03 or 21, you will be alerted that the transaction should not be resubmitted. A value of 03 indicates that the cardholder account has been closed or is fraudulent. A value of 21 also indicates a closed cardholder account.
To reinforce this requirement, Mastercard will begin charging a fee for resubmitted code 03 and 21 transactions. This fee will be passed to your business beginning in April 2022. Any fees your business incurs will be reflected on your payment processing account beginning with your April 2022 billing statement.
**What is the process for technical support?**
The EPG Helpdesk is the dedicated technical team for EPG and Plugins:
**EPG Helpdesk**
- Email: **North America**:
- Telephone: **USA**: +1 800 377 3962 (24/7)
